{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-fable-5","id":"msg_01SmPgZiGpg5STabxzWzPXge","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":752,"cache_read_input_tokens":34985,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":752,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":30,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"9777808e-4b15-489a-a904-14dbfb0b648f","uuid":"bf065b99-88f0-464f-9da5-057f9a92c23d","request_id":"req_011CbwMy7fvkaPQzSBkuq67m"}
{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-fable-5","id":"msg_01SmPgZiGpg5STabxzWzPXge","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01CHWLbq6M39YYvwRgoJiXuc","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/app/parse_data.py","content":"\"\"\"Parse all raw documents and reconstruct wholesaler->store FIFO flows.\"\"\"\nimport json, os, re, pickle\nfrom collections import defaultdict, deque\n\nDAYS = range(1, 366)\n\ndef parse_items_file(path):\n    \"\"\"Return dict product -> qty from any of the 4 formats.\"\"\"\n    items = {}\n    if path.endswith('.json'):\n        with open(path) as f:\n            d = json.load(f)\n        for it in d['items']:\n            qty = it.get('qty', it.get('delivered_qty'))\n            items[it['prod_id']] = int(qty)\n    elif path.endswith('.csv'):\n        with open(path) as f:\n            for line in f:\n                line = line.strip()\n                if not line or line.startswith('PRODUCT'):\n                    continue\n                p, q = line.split(';')\n                items[p] = int(q)\n    else:\n        with open(path) as f:\n            text = f.read()\n        if 'ORDER_PRODUCT' in text or 'DELIVERED_QTY' in text:\n            # key-value\n            for m in re.finditer(r'(?:ORDER_PRODUCT|PRODUCT): (P_\\d+) \\| (?:ORDER_QTY|DELIVERED_QTY): (\\d+)', text):\n                items[m.group(1)] = int(m.group(2))\n        else:\n            # text-template\n            for m in re.finditer(r'deliver(?:ed)? (\\d+) units of product (P_\\d+)', text):\n                items[m.group(2)] = int(m.group(1))\n    return items\n\n# ---- orders: (day, store) -> {product: qty}\norders = {}\nfor fn in os.listdir('raw_orders'):\n    m = re.match(r'day_(\\d+)_(S_\\d+)_', fn)\n    day, store = int(m.group(1)), m.group(2)\n    orders[(day, store)] = parse_items_file('raw_orders/' + fn)\n\n# ---- supplier receipts: (day, producer) -> {product: qty}\nsupplier = {}\nfor fn in os.listdir('raw_supplier_receipts'):\n    m = re.match(r'day_(\\d+)_(PR_\\d+)_', fn)\n    day, pr = int(m.group(1)), m.group(2)\n    supplier[(day, pr)] = parse_items_file('raw_supplier_receipts/' + fn)\n\n# ---- wholesaler receipts: day -> [producer sorted by seq]\nprod_seq = defaultdict(list)\nfor fn in os.listdir('raw_wholesaler_receipts'):\n    with open('raw_wholesaler_receipts/' + fn) as f:\n        t = f.read()\n    day = int(re.search(r'DAY: (\\d+)', t).group(1))\n    sup = re.search(r'SUPPLIER_ID: (PR_\\d+)', t).group(1)\n    seq = int(re.search(r'DAILY_SEQUENCE_NO: (\\d+)', t).group(1))\n    prod_seq[day].append((seq, sup))\nfor day in prod_seq:\n    prod_seq[day].sort()\n\n# ---- store receipts: day -> [(store, total) sorted by seq]\nstore_seq = defaultdict(list)\nfor fn in os.listdir('raw_wholesaler_store_receipts'):\n    with open('raw_wholesaler_store_receipts/' + fn) as f:\n        t = f.read()\n    day = int(re.search(r'DAY: (\\d+)', t).group(1))\n    st = re.search(r'STORE_ID: (S_\\d+)', t).group(1)\n    seq = int(re.search(r'DAILY_SEQUENCE_NO: (\\d+)', t).group(1))\n    tot = int(re.search(r'TOTAL_DELIVERED_UNITS: (\\d+)', t).group(1))\n    store_seq[day].append((seq, st, tot))\nfor day in store_seq:\n    store_seq[day].sort()\n\nprint('orders:', len(orders), 'supplier:', len(supplier),\n      'days w/ prod seq:', len(prod_seq), 'days w/ store seq:', len(store_seq))\n\n# ---- simulate wholesaler FIFO ----\n# wh[product] = deque of [producer, qty] lots in arrival order\nwh = defaultdict(deque)\n# deliveries[(day, store)][product] = list of (producer, qty) in FIFO order\ndeliveries = defaultdict(lambda: defaultdict(list))\nmismatch = 0\nfor day in DAYS:\n    # producers deliver in sequence order\n    for seq, pr in prod_seq.get(day, []):\n        for prod, qty in supplier.get((day, pr), {}).items():\n            wh[prod].append([pr, qty])\n    # stores fulfilled in sequence order\n    for seq, st, tot in store_seq.get(day, []):\n        order = orders.get((day, st), {})\n        delivered_total = 0\n        for prod, want in order.items():\n            q = wh[prod]\n            got = []\n            need = want\n            while need > 0 and q:\n                lot = q[0]\n                take = min(need, lot[1])\n                got.append((lot[0], take))\n                lot[1] -= take\n                need -= take\n                if lot[1] == 0:\n                    q.popleft()\n            if got:\n                deliveries[(day, st)][prod] = got\n                delivered_total += sum(g[1] for g in got)\n        if delivered_total != tot:\n            mismatch += 1\n            if mismatch <= 10:\n                print(f'MISMATCH day {day} {st}: model {delivered_total} receipt {tot}')\nprint('total store-day delivery mismatches:', mismatch)\n\nwith open('flows.pkl', 'wb') as f:\n    pickle.dump({'orders': orders, 'supplier': supplier,\n                 'deliveries': {k: dict(v) for k, v in deliveries.items()}}, f)\nprint('saved flows.pkl')\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":752,"cache_read_input_tokens":34985,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":752,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":30,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"9777808e-4b15-489a-a904-14dbfb0b648f","uuid":"d0499c63-c510-40d4-aea6-0bd773d61c91","request_id":"req_011CbwMy7fvkaPQzSBkuq67m"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-fable-5","id":"msg_01AxF7gJM7ZXQRfiQAgv333k","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01EdZBqXZB9LHEY4gnWA2uir","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/app/store_sim.py","content":"\"\"\"Simulate store FIFO inventory; figure out which producer units were sold\neach (day, store, product). Validates timing assumptions against Lost_Sales.\"\"\"\nimport pickle, pandas as pd\nfrom collections import defaultdict, deque\n\nwith open('flows.pkl', 'rb') as f:\n    flows = pickle.load(f)\ndeliveries = flows['deliveries']\n\nsales = pd.read_csv('sales_log.csv')\nday_sales = sales.groupby(['Day', 'Store_ID', 'Product_ID']).agg(\n    demand=('Reported_Demand', 'sum'), sold=('Sold_Quantity', 'sum'),\n    lost=('Lost_Sales', 'sum')).reset_index()\n\nstores = sorted(sales['Store_ID'].unique(), key=lambda s: int(s.split('_')[1]))\nproducts = sorted(sales['Product_ID'].unique(), key=lambda s: int(s.split('_')[1]))\nDAYS = range(1, 366)\n\nsold_map = {(r.Day, r.Store_ID, r.Product_ID): (r.sold, r.lost, r.demand)\n            for r in day_sales.itertuples()}\n\n# inv[(store, product)] = deque of [producer, qty, wholesaler_day]\ninv = defaultdict(deque)\n# consumed[(day, store, product)] = list of (producer, qty, wh_day)\nconsumed = {}\nerrors = 0\nlost_with_stock = 0\nfor day in DAYS:\n    # sales happen first (inventory delivered up to day-1)\n    for st in stores:\n        for prod in products:\n            sold, lost, demand = sold_map.get((day, st, prod), (0, 0, 0))\n            q = inv[(st, prod)]\n            need = sold\n            got = []\n            while need > 0 and q:\n                lot = q[0]\n                take = min(need, lot[1])\n                got.append((lot[0], take, lot[2]))\n                lot[1] -= take\n                need -= take\n                if lot[1] == 0:\n                    q.popleft()\n            if need > 0:\n                errors += 1\n                if errors <= 5:\n                    print(f'NEG INV day {day} {st} {prod}: sold {sold}, short {need}')\n            if lost > 0 and q:\n                lost_with_stock += 1\n                if lost_with_stock <= 5:\n                    rem = sum(l[1] for l in q)\n                    print(f'LOST WITH STOCK day {day} {st} {prod}: lost {lost}, remaining {rem}')\n            if got:\n                consumed[(day, st, prod)] = got\n    # evening: today's delivery arrives\n    for st in stores:\n        for prod, lots in deliveries.get((day, st), {}).items():\n            for pr, qty in lots:\n                inv[(st, prod)].append([pr, qty, day])\n\nprint('neg-inventory errors:', errors, '| lost-sales-despite-stock:', lost_with_stock)\nwith open('consumed.pkl', 'wb') as f:\n    pickle.dump(consumed, f)\nday_sales.to_pickle('day_sales.pkl')\nprint('saved consumed.pkl, day_sales.pkl')\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":131,"cache_creation_input_tokens":160,"cache_read_input_tokens":38116,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":160,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":33,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"9777808e-4b15-489a-a904-14dbfb0b648f","uuid":"cc2df90d-56c3-4269-abf7-898ae6f15f30","request_id":"req_011CbwN2LJH9KP57k95dfaye"}

sales happen first (inventory delivered up to day-1)\n    for st in stores:\n        for prod in products:\n            sold, lost, demand = sold_map.get((day, st, prod), (0, 0, 0))\n            q = inv[(st, prod)]\n            need = sold\n            got = []\n            while need > 0 and q:\n                lot = q[0]\n                take = min(need, lot[1])\n                got.append((lot[0], take, lot[2]))\n                lot[1] -= take\n                need -= take\n                if lot[1] == 0:\n                    q.popleft()\n            if need > 0:\n                errors += 1\n                if errors <= 5:\n                    print(f'NEG INV day {day} {st} {prod}: sold {sold}, short {need}')\n            if lost > 0 and q:\n                lost_with_stock += 1\n                if lost_with_stock <= 5:\n                    rem = sum(l[1] for l in q)\n                    print(f'LOST WITH STOCK day {day} {st} {prod}: lost {lost}, remaining {rem}')\n            if got:\n                consumed[(day, st, prod)] = got\n    # evening: today's delivery arrives\n    for st in stores:\n        for prod, lots in deliveries.get((day, st), {}).items():\n            for pr, qty in lots:\n                inv[(st, prod)].append([pr, qty, day])\n\nprint('neg-inventory errors:', errors, '| lost-sales-despite-stock:', lost_with_stock)\nwith open('consumed.pkl', 'wb') as f:\n    pickle.dump(consumed, f)\nday_sales.to_pickle('day_sales.pkl')\nprint('saved consumed.pkl, day_sales.pkl')\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null,"userModified":false}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-fable-5","id":"msg_01CusdxJPptRTpa6Gwvwtjcv","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01A9sgr2VFkGjV9bX1ufKRhw","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"python3 << 'EOF'\nimport pandas as pd, numpy as np\nds = pd.read_pickle('day_sales.pkl')\npiv = ds.pivot_table(index='Day', columns=['Product_ID','Store_ID'], values='demand', fill_value=0)\n\n# per (product, store) normalized by product-day cross-store median ratio\n# step 1: base level per (p,s) = median over year\nresults = []\nfor p in piv.columns.levels[0]:\n    sub = piv[p]  # day x store\n    base = sub.median(axis=0)\n    norm = sub / base  # ratio\n    cal = norm.median(axis=1)  # robust calendar per product\n    resid = norm.div(cal, axis=0)\n    results.append((p, resid))\n\n# look at residual distributions\nallres = pd.concat([r for _, r in results], axis=1)\nprint('residual quantiles:', np.nanquantile(allres.values, [0.001, 0.01, 0.05, 0.5, 0.95, 0.99, 0.999]).round(3))\n\n# find strong dips: residual < 0.85\ncnt = (allres.values < 0.85).sum()\nprint('day-store-product points with resid<0.85:', cnt, 'of', allres.size)\n\n# example: show one dip\np, r = results[0]\nlow = r[r < 0.8].stack()\nprint('\\nP_1 days with resid<0.8 (first 20):')\nprint(low.head(20))\nEOF","description":"Normalize demand and scan for dip events"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":3531,"cache_read_input_tokens":40203,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":3531,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":30,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"9777808e-4b15-489a-a904-14dbfb0b648f","uuid":"c6d61560-1127-4883-9769-f62fa46de54d","request_id":"req_011CbwN7yTpBEs6TPANLYFHC"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-fable-5","id":"msg_01UaWs816h961zNmV68PD8WB","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_011W5bo94BKS8jZUTDxFzziW","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/app/regress.py","content":"\"\"\"Pooled regression: demand deficit ~ producer exposure (recovery kernel).\"\"\"\nimport pandas as pd, numpy as np, pickle\n\nwith open('consumed.pkl','rb') as f: consumed = pickle.load(f)\nds = pd.read_pickle('day_sales.pkl')\npiv = ds.pivot_table(index='Day', columns=['Product_ID','Store_ID'], values='demand', fill_value=0)\n\nproducts = sorted(piv.columns.levels[0], key=lambda s:int(s.split('_')[1]))\nstores = sorted(piv[products[0]].columns, key=lambda s:int(s.split('_')[1]))\nproducers = [f'PR_{i}' for i in range(1,61)]\npr_idx = {p:i for i,p in enumerate(producers)}\nst_idx = {s:i for i,s in enumerate(stores)}\nT = 365\n\n# units sold per (product, store, day, producer)\nU = {p: np.zeros((10, T+1, 60)) for p in products}\nfor (day, st, prod), lots in consumed.items():\n    for pr, qty, whd in lots:\n        U[prod][st_idx[st], day, pr_idx[pr]] += qty\n\n# demand, baseline, calendar\nresults = {}\nkernel = np.array([0.8]*3 + [0.5]*4 + [0.2]*7)\n\nfor align in (0, 1):  # 0: dip starts same day as purchase; 1: starts next day\n    print(f'=== alignment lag start {align} ===')\n    r2s, betas = [], {}\n    for p in products:\n        D = piv[p][stores].values  # (365, 10) demand, day index 0->day1\n        base = np.median(D, axis=0)            # per store\n        norm = D / base\n        cal = np.median(norm, axis=1)          # per day\n        ratio = norm / cal[:, None]\n        y = (1 - ratio).T.reshape(-1)          # store-major (10*365)\n        # exposure: x[X][s,t] = sum_lag k * u(s, t-lag) / (base*cal at sale day)\n        X = np.zeros((10*T, 60))\n        u = U[p]  # (10, T+1, 60)\n        for s in range(10):\n            scale = base[s]*cal  # expected demand per day, len 365\n            un = u[s,1:,:] / scale[:,None]     # normalized units sold (day idx 0=day1)\n            for li, k in enumerate(kernel):\n                lag = align + li\n                if lag == 0:\n                    X[s*T:(s+1)*T, :] += k*un\n                else:\n                    X[s*T+lag:(s+1)*T, :] += k*un[:-lag]\n        # drop first 20 days (startup) per store\n        mask = np.ones(10*T, bool)\n        for s in range(10):\n            mask[s*T:s*T+20] = False\n        Xm, ym = X[mask], y[mask]\n        Xd = np.column_stack([np.ones(len(ym)), Xm])\n        coef, res, *_ = np.linalg.lstsq(Xd, ym, rcond=None)\n        pred = Xd@coef\n        ssr = ((ym-pred)**2).sum(); sst = ((ym-ym.mean())**2).sum()\n        r2s.append(1-ssr/sst)\n        betas[p] = coef[1:]\n    print('mean R2 over products:', np.mean(r2s).round(4))\n    if align==1:\n        np.save('betas_align1.npy', np.array([betas[p] for p in products]))\n    else:\n        np.save('betas_align0.npy', np.array([betas[p] for p in products]))\n\nwith open('design_meta.pkl','wb') as f:\n    pickle.dump({'products':products,'stores':stores,'producers':producers}, f)\nprint('done')\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1473,"cache_read_input_tokens":51981,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":1473,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":40,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"9777808e-4b15-489a-a904-14dbfb0b648f","uuid":"f87a09fe-bd0c-42b2-908e-3c054654f274","request_id":"req_011CbwNJpAXBKMXMun893fTi"}

sst = ((ym-ym.mean())**2).sum()\n        r2s.append(1-ssr/sst)\n        betas[p] = coef[1:]\n    print('mean R2 over products:', np.mean(r2s).round(4))\n    if align==1:\n        np.save('betas_align1.npy', np.array([betas[p] for p in products]))\n    else:\n        np.save('betas_align0.npy', np.array([betas[p] for p in products]))\n\nwith open('design_meta.pkl','wb') as f:\n    pickle.dump({'products':products,'stores':stores,'producers':producers}, f)\nprint('done')\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null,"userModified":false}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-fable-5","id":"msg_01HBuzfoRVb7nCbQkFUmUdzs","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_016PaSjgu1CpPdqWhxDpXWjf","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/app/batch_scores.py","content":"\"\"\"Per-batch matched-filter z-scores.\nBatch = (product, producer, wholesaler_day). Exposure = its units sold at\n(store, day), convolved with recovery kernel (dip starts day after purchase).\"\"\"\nimport pandas as pd, numpy as np, pickle\nfrom collections import defaultdict\n\nwith open('consumed.pkl','rb') as f: consumed = pickle.load(f)\nds = pd.read_pickle('day_sales.pkl')\npiv = ds.pivot_table(index='Day', columns=['Product_ID','Store_ID'], values='demand', fill_value=0)\nproducts = sorted(piv.columns.levels[0], key=lambda s:int(s.split('_')[1]))\nstores = sorted(piv[products[0]].columns, key=lambda s:int(s.split('_')[1]))\nst_idx = {s:i for i,s in enumerate(stores)}\nT = 365\nkernel = np.array([0.8]*3 + [0.5]*4 + [0.2]*7)  # lags 1..14\n\n# deficit y and expected demand per product\nY, EXP = {}, {}\nfor p in products:\n    D = piv[p][stores].values\n    base = np.median(D, axis=0)\n    norm = D/base\n    cal = np.median(norm, axis=1)\n    exp_d = base[None,:]*cal[:,None]      # (365,10)\n    Y[p] = (1 - D/exp_d).T                # (10,365) deficit\n    EXP[p] = exp_d.T                      # (10,365)\n\n# noise: residual sd should scale ~ c/sqrt(exp_d); calibrate c robustly\nallz = []\nfor p in products:\n    y, e = Y[p], EXP[p]\n    allz.append((y*np.sqrt(e)).ravel())\nallz = np.concatenate(allz)\nc = 1.4826*np.median(np.abs(allz - np.median(allz)))\nprint('noise scale c =', round(c,3), '(deficit sd = c/sqrt(expected demand))')\n\n# group consumed lots by batch\nbatch_sales = defaultdict(list)   # (product, producer, whd) -> [(store_idx, day, units)]\nfor (day, st, prod), lots in consumed.items():\n    for pr, qty, whd in lots:\n        batch_sales[(prod, pr, whd)].append((st_idx[st], day, qty))\n\nrows = []\nfor (prod, pr, whd), sl in batch_sales.items():\n    y, e = Y[prod], EXP[prod]\n    num = 0.0; den = 0.0\n    units = 0\n    for s, d, q in sl:\n        units += q\n        f = q/e[s, d-1]                   # affected-consumer fraction proxy\n        for li, k in enumerate(kernel):\n            t = d + 1 + li                # day index (1-based); dip starts next day\n            if t > T: break\n            x = k*f\n            w = e[s, t-1]/(c*c)           # 1/sigma^2\n            num += y[s, t-1]*x*w\n            den += x*x*w\n    if den > 0:\n        rows.append((prod, pr, whd, units, num/den, num/np.sqrt(den)))\n\nbs = pd.DataFrame(rows, columns=['product','producer','whd','units','beta','z'])\nbs.to_pickle('batch_scores.pkl')\nprint(bs.shape)\nprint('z quantiles:', np.quantile(bs.z,[0.001,0.01,0.5,0.99,0.999]).round(2))\nprint('\\ntop 30 by z:')\nprint(bs.sort_values('z',ascending=False).head(30).to_string())\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":131,"cache_creation_input_tokens":2996,"cache_read_input_tokens":57098,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":2996,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":28,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"9777808e-4b15-489a-a904-14dbfb0b648f","uuid":"97f17e9b-7722-47e1-a0da-300f26d3db67","request_id":"req_011CbwNR6cA4QB5o2ynJajuX"}

dip starts next day\n            if t > T: break\n            x = k*f\n            w = e[s, t-1]/(c*c)           # 1/sigma^2\n            num += y[s, t-1]*x*w\n            den += x*x*w\n    if den > 0:\n        rows.append((prod, pr, whd, units, num/den, num/np.sqrt(den)))\n\nbs = pd.DataFrame(rows, columns=['product','producer','whd','units','beta','z'])\nbs.to_pickle('batch_scores.pkl')\nprint(bs.shape)\nprint('z quantiles:', np.quantile(bs.z,[0.001,0.01,0.5,0.99,0.999]).round(2))\nprint('\\ntop 30 by z:')\nprint(bs.sort_values('z',ascending=False).head(30).to_string())\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null,"userModified":false}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-fable-5","id":"msg_01TcErGbmxRF6vS6AQ6DCUTN","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_014miYDjQW3146rpUXREtspM","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/app/omp_fit.py","content":"\"\"\"Joint sparse attribution: per product, greedy OMP over batch exposure\nvectors with nonnegative least-squares refit.\"\"\"\nimport pandas as pd, numpy as np, pickle\nfrom collections import defaultdict\nfrom scipy.optimize import nnls\n\nwith open('consumed.pkl','rb') as f: consumed = pickle.load(f)\nds = pd.read_pickle('day_sales.pkl')\npiv = ds.pivot_table(index='Day', columns=['Product_ID','Store_ID'], values='demand', fill_value=0)\nproducts = sorted(piv.columns.levels[0], key=lambda s:int(s.split('_')[1]))\nstores = sorted(piv[products[0]].columns, key=lambda s:int(s.split('_')[1]))\nst_idx = {s:i for i,s in enumerate(stores)}\nT = 365\nkernel = np.array([0.8]*3 + [0.5]*4 + [0.2]*7)\nC = 0.336  # noise scale from calibration\n\nbatch_sales = defaultdict(list)\nfor (day, st, prod), lots in consumed.items():\n    for pr, qty, whd in lots:\n        batch_sales[(prod, pr, whd)].append((st_idx[st], day, qty))\n\nselected_all = []\nfor prod in products:\n    D = piv[prod][stores].values\n    base = np.median(D, axis=0); norm = D/base\n    cal = np.median(norm, axis=1)\n    exp_d = (base[None,:]*cal[:,None]).T        # (10,365)\n    y = (1 - (D/ (base[None,:]*cal[:,None])).T) # (10,365) deficit\n    sig = C/np.sqrt(exp_d)\n    w = 1.0/sig\n    yw = (y*w).ravel()\n\n    # build whitened sparse exposure vectors per batch\n    keys, cols = [], []\n    for (pp, pr, whd), sl in batch_sales.items():\n        if pp != prod: continue\n        idx, val = [], []\n        for s, d, q in sl:\n            f = q/exp_d[s, d-1]\n            for li, k in enumerate(kernel):\n                t = d+1+li\n                if t > T: break\n                idx.append(s*T + (t-1)); val.append(k*f*w[s, t-1])\n        if not idx: continue\n        idx = np.array(idx); val = np.array(val)\n        # merge duplicate cells\n        o = np.argsort(idx); idx, val = idx[o], val[o]\n        uidx, inv = np.unique(idx, return_inverse=True)\n        uval = np.zeros(len(uidx)); np.add.at(uval, inv, val)\n        keys.append((pr, whd)); cols.append((uidx, uval))\n    nb = len(keys)\n    norms = np.array([np.sqrt((v**2).sum()) for _, v in cols])\n\n    r = yw.copy()\n    active = []\n    for it in range(80):\n        zs = np.array([ (r[i]*v).sum() for i, v in cols ]) / norms\n        for a in active: zs[a] = -np.inf\n        j = int(np.argmax(zs))\n        if zs[j] < 5.0: break\n        active.append(j)\n        A = np.zeros((len(yw), len(active)))\n        for ci, a in enumerate(active):\n            i, v = cols[a]; A[i, ci] = v\n        coef, _ = nnls(A, yw)\n        r = yw - A@coef\n    if active:\n        # final joint z per active batch: drop-one delta\n        A = np.zeros((len(yw), len(active)))\n        for ci, a in enumerate(active):\n            i, v = cols[a]; A[i, ci] = v\n        coef, _ = nnls(A, yw)\n        sse_full = ((yw - A@coef)**2).sum()\n        for ci, a in enumerate(active):\n            if coef[ci] <= 0: continue\n            keep = [k for k in range(len(active)) if k != ci]\n            if keep:\n                c2, _ = nnls(A[:, keep], yw)\n                sse_red = ((yw - A[:,keep]@c2)**2).sum()\n            else:\n                sse_red = (yw**2).sum()\n            dz = np.sqrt(max(sse_red - sse_full, 0))\n            pr, whd = keys[a]\n            selected_all.append((prod, pr, whd, coef[ci], dz))\n    print(prod, 'selected', sum(1 for s in selected_all if s[0]==prod))\n\nsel = pd.DataFrame(selected_all, columns=['product','producer','whd','coef','dropz'])\nsel.to_pickle('omp_selected.pkl')\nprint(sel.sort_values(['product','whd']).to_string())\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":967,"cache_read_input_tokens":66430,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":967,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":36,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"9777808e-4b15-489a-a904-14dbfb0b648f","uuid":"a70f47be-c72f-4240-9d7f-9277f5a574cc","request_id":"req_011CbwNYqBBsj9PpSSJn6GKK"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-fable-5","id":"msg_01RoTLLADWJcbMZsAYGTDDRF","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_012FJkfwYpYncqBVAL1sHZvS","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"python3 << 'EOF'\nimport pandas as pd, numpy as np, pickle\nfrom collections import defaultdict\nfrom scipy.optimize import nnls\n\nwith open('consumed.pkl','rb') as f: consumed = pickle.load(f)\nds = pd.read_pickle('day_sales.pkl')\npiv = ds.pivot_table(index='Day', columns=['Product_ID','Store_ID'], values='demand', fill_value=0)\nproducts = sorted(piv.columns.levels[0], key=lambda s:int(s.split('_')[1]))\nstores = sorted(piv[products[0]].columns, key=lambda s:int(s.split('_')[1]))\nst_idx = {s:i for i,s in enumerate(stores)}\nT=365; kernel = np.array([0.8]*3+[0.5]*4+[0.2]*7); C=0.336\n\nbatch_sales = defaultdict(list)\nfor (day, st, prod), lots in consumed.items():\n    for pr, qty, whd in lots:\n        batch_sales[(prod, pr, whd)].append((st_idx[st], day, qty))\n\nout=[]\nfor prod in products[:10]:  # 10 products enough for null calibration\n    D = piv[prod][stores].values\n    base = np.median(D, axis=0); cal = np.median(D/base, axis=1)\n    exp_d = (base[None,:]*cal[:,None]).T\n    y = -(1 - (D/(base[None,:]*cal[:,None])).T)   # NEGATED: fit excess\n    w = np.sqrt(exp_d)/C\n    yw = (y*w).ravel()\n    keys, cols = [], []\n    for (pp, pr, whd), sl in batch_sales.items():\n        if pp != prod: continue\n        idx, val = [], []\n        for s, d, q in sl:\n            f = q/exp_d[s, d-1]\n            for li,k in enumerate(kernel):\n                t = d+1+li\n                if t>T: break\n                idx.append(s*T+t-1); val.append(k*f*w[s,t-1])\n        if not idx: continue\n        idx=np.array(idx); val=np.array(val)\n        o=np.argsort(idx); idx,val=idx[o],val[o]\n        ui,inv=np.unique(idx,return_inverse=True)\n        uv=np.zeros(len(ui)); np.add.at(uv,inv,val)\n        keys.append((pr,whd)); cols.append((ui,uv))\n    norms = np.array([np.sqrt((v**2).sum()) for _,v in cols])\n    r = yw.copy(); active=[]\n    for it in range(40):\n        zs = np.array([(r[i]*v).sum() for i,v in cols])/norms\n        for a in active: zs[a]=-np.inf\n        j=int(np.argmax(zs))\n        if zs[j] < 4.0: break\n        active.append(j)\n        A=np.zeros((len(yw),len(active)))\n        for ci,a in enumerate(active):\n            i,v=cols[a]; A[i,ci]=v\n        coef,_=nnls(A,yw); r=yw-A@coef\n    if active:\n        A=np.zeros((len(yw),len(active)))\n        for ci,a in enumerate(active):\n            i,v=cols[a]; A[i,ci]=v\n        coef,_=nnls(A,yw)\n        ssef=((yw-A@coef)**2).sum()\n        for ci,a in enumerate(active):\n            if coef[ci]<=0: continue\n            keep=[k for k in range(len(active)) if k!=ci]\n            c2,_=nnls(A[:,keep],yw) if keep else (np.zeros(0),0)\n            sser=((yw-A[:,keep]@c2)**2).sum() if keep else (yw**2).sum()\n            out.append((prod,keys[a][0],keys[a][1],coef[ci],np.sqrt(max(sser-ssef,0))))\n    print(prod,'null-selected:',len([o for o in out if o[0]==prod]))\nneg = pd.DataFrame(out,columns=['product','producer','whd','coef','dropz'])\nprint(neg.sort_values('dropz',ascending=False).head(20).to_string())\nprint('\\nnull dropz quantiles:', np.quantile(neg.dropz,[0.5,0.9,0.99,1.0]).round(2) if len(neg) else 'none')\nEOF","description":"Negative control: OMP on demand excess to calibrate null"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":2404,"cache_read_input_tokens":74335,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":2404,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":52,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"9777808e-4b15-489a-a904-14dbfb0b648f","uuid":"4099f78d-5eb5-4ac4-b232-ee9f4481b049","request_id":"req_011CbwNi7fNrmGkFzBVcV3Wj"}

30-day moving average range per store\nimport numpy as np\nma = pd.DataFrame(resid).rolling(30,center=True).mean()\nprint('30d-MA range per store:', (ma.max()-ma.min()).round(3).tolist())\n# month-level store profile\nm = pd.DataFrame(resid, index=piv.index)\nm['month']=((m.index-1)//30)\nprof = m.groupby('month').mean()\nprint('\\nmonthly mean residual ratio per store (P_5):')\nprint(prof.round(3).to_string())\nEOF","description":"Check residual autocorrelation and store-level drift"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":3358,"cache_read_input_tokens":76739,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":3358,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":29,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"9777808e-4b15-489a-a904-14dbfb0b648f","uuid":"ccf89f92-90bd-4691-b13d-c1b0e3990fe7","request_id":"req_011CbwNmJkDF1sDUVs6znc6C"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-fable-5","id":"msg_01H2DAeV4rx8CjXB6ZWba7cx","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01KwwEYvVMVsShL2F1yfMtWU","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/app/model2.py","content":"\"\"\"Improved demand model + OMP attribution + negative control.\n\nBaseline: cal_p(t) (cross-store robust) x L(s,p,t) (rolling-median local level,\niteratively refit with detected dips masked). AR(1) prewhitening. OMP over\nbatch exposures; same machinery run on negated deficit as a null calibration.\n\"\"\"\nimport pandas as pd, numpy as np, pickle, sys\nfrom collections import defaultdict\nfrom scipy.optimize import nnls\n\nNEG = '--neg' in sys.argv\n\nwith open('consumed.pkl','rb') as f: consumed = pickle.load(f)\nds = pd.read_pickle('day_sales.pkl')\npiv = ds.pivot_table(index='Day', columns=['Product_ID','Store_ID'], values='demand', fill_value=0)\nproducts = sorted(piv.columns.levels[0], key=lambda s:int(s.split('_')[1]))\nstores = sorted(piv[products[0]].columns, key=lambda s:int(s.split('_')[1]))\nst_idx = {s:i for i,s in enumerate(stores)}\nT = 365\nkernel = np.array([0.8]*3 + [0.5]*4 + [0.2]*7)\n\nbatch_sales = defaultdict(list)\nfor (day, st, prod), lots in consumed.items():\n    for pr, qty, whd in lots:\n        batch_sales[(prod, pr, whd)].append((st_idx[st], day, qty))\n\n\ndef rolling_median_masked(x, mask, win=41):\n    \"\"\"centered rolling median ignoring masked entries\"\"\"\n    n = len(x); h = win//2\n    out = np.empty(n)\n    for i in range(n):\n        lo, hi = max(0, i-h), min(n, i+h+1)\n        seg = x[lo:hi][~mask[lo:hi]]\n        out[i] = np.median(seg) if len(seg) >= 8 else np.nan\n    # fill nans\n    s = pd.Series(out).ffill().bfill().values\n    return s\n\n\ndef build_y(prod):\n    D = piv[prod][stores].values.astype(float)  # (365,10)\n    base = np.median(D, axis=0)\n    cal = np.median(D/base, axis=1)\n    E = np.empty_like(D)\n    for s in range(10):\n        r0 = D[:, s]/cal\n        mask = np.zeros(T, bool)\n        for _ in range(3):\n            L = rolling_median_masked(r0, mask)\n            e = cal*L\n            y = 1 - D[:, s]/e\n            sig = 0.34/np.sqrt(e)\n            mask = y > 2.5*sig          # mask probable dips, refit level\n        E[:, s] = cal*L\n    y = (1 - D/E).T                      # (10,365)\n    return y, E.T\n\n\n# calibrate noise + AR(1) on leveled residuals\nzs_all = []\nys, Es = {}, {}\nfor prod in products:\n    y, E = build_y(prod)\n    ys[prod], Es[prod] = y, E\n    zs_all.append((y*np.sqrt(E)).ravel())\nzs_all = np.concatenate(zs_all)\nc = 1.4826*np.median(np.abs(zs_all-np.median(zs_all)))\n# AR(1) on standardized residuals (clip extremes to limit dip influence)\nrho_n, rho_d = 0.0, 0.0\nfor prod in products:\n    z = ys[prod]*np.sqrt(Es[prod])/c\n    z = np.clip(z, -3, 3)\n    rho_n += (z[:, 1:]*z[:, :-1]).sum(); rho_d += (z[:, :-1]**2).sum()\nrho = rho_n/rho_d\nprint('noise c =', round(c, 3), ' AR1 rho =', round(rho, 3))\n\nresults = []\nfor prod in products:\n    y, E = ys[prod], Es[prod]\n    if NEG:\n        y = -y\n    w = np.sqrt(E)/c\n    yw = y*w\n    # AR(1) prewhiten along time within each store\n    yw2 = yw.copy(); yw2[:, 1:] -= rho*yw[:, :-1]\n    ywf = yw2.ravel()\n\n    keys, cols = [], []\n    for (pp, pr, whd), sl in batch_sales.items():\n        if pp != prod: continue\n        grid = {}\n        for s, d, q in sl:\n            f = q/E[s, d-1]\n            for li, k in enumerate(kernel):\n                t = d+1+li\n                if t > T: break\n                grid[(s, t-1)] = grid.get((s, t-1), 0.) + k*f*w[s, t-1]\n        if not grid: continue\n        # prewhiten exposure same way\n        g2 = {}\n        for (s, ti), v in grid.items():\n            g2[(s, ti)] = g2.get((s, ti), 0.) + v\n            if ti+1 < T:\n                g2[(s, ti+1)] = g2.get((s, ti+1), 0.) - rho*v\n        idx = np.array([s*T+ti for (s, ti) in g2])\n        val = np.array(list(g2.values()))\n        o = np.argsort(idx)\n        keys.append((pr, whd)); cols.append((idx[o], val[o]))\n    norms = np.array([np.sqrt((v**2).sum()) for _, v in cols])\n\n    r = ywf.copy(); active = []\n    for it in range(60):\n        zsc = np.array([(r[i]*v).sum() for i, v in cols])/norms\n        for a in active: zsc[a] = -np.inf\n        j = int(np.argmax(zsc))\n        if zsc[j] < 4.5: break\n        active.append(j)\n        A = np.zeros((len(ywf), len(active)))\n        for ci, a in enumerate(active):\n            i, v = cols[a]; A[i, ci] = v\n        coef, _ = nnls(A, ywf)\n        r = ywf - A@coef\n    if active:\n        A = np.zeros((len(ywf), len(active)))\n        for ci, a in enumerate(active):\n            i, v = cols[a]; AR(1) prewhitening. OMP over\nbatch exposures; same machinery run on negated deficit as a null calibration.\n\"\"\"\nimport pandas as pd, numpy as np, pickle, sys\nfrom collections import defaultdict\nfrom scipy.optimize import nnls\n\nNEG = '--neg' in sys.argv\n\nwith open('consumed.pkl','rb') as f: consumed = pickle.load(f)\nds = pd.read_pickle('day_sales.pkl')\npiv = ds.pivot_table(index='Day', columns=['Product_ID','Store_ID'], values='demand', fill_value=0)\nproducts = sorted(piv.columns.levels[0], key=lambda s:int(s.split('_')[1]))\nstores = sorted(piv[products[0]].columns, key=lambda s:int(s.split('_')[1]))\nst_idx = {s:i for i,s in enumerate(stores)}\nT = 365\nkernel = np.array([0.8]*3 + [0.5]*4 + [0.2]*7)\n\nbatch_sales = defaultdict(list)\nfor (day, st, prod), lots in consumed.items():\n    for pr, qty, whd in lots:\n        batch_sales[(prod, pr, whd)].append((st_idx[st], day, qty))\n\n\ndef rolling_median_masked(x, mask, win=41):\n    \"\"\"centered rolling median ignoring masked entries\"\"\"\n    n = len(x); h = win//2\n    out = np.empty(n)\n    for i in range(n):\n        lo, hi = max(0, i-h), min(n, i+h+1)\n        seg = x[lo:hi][~mask[lo:hi]]\n        out[i] = np.median(seg) if len(seg) >= 8 else np.nan\n    # fill nans\n    s = pd.Series(out).ffill().bfill().values\n    return s\n\n\ndef build_y(prod):\n    D = piv[prod][stores].values.astype(float)  # (365,10)\n    base = np.median(D, axis=0)\n    cal = np.median(D/base, axis=1)\n    E = np.empty_like(D)\n    for s in range(10):\n        r0 = D[:, s]/cal\n        mask = np.zeros(T, bool)\n        for _ in range(3):\n            L = rolling_median_masked(r0, mask)\n            e = cal*L\n            y = 1 - D[:, s]/e\n            sig = 0.34/np.sqrt(e)\n            mask = y > 2.5*sig          # mask probable dips, refit level\n        E[:, s] = cal*L\n    y = (1 - D/E).T                      # (10,365)\n    return y, E.T\n\n\n# calibrate noise + AR(1) on leveled residuals\nzs_all = []\nys, Es = {}, {}\nfor prod in products:\n    y, E = build_y(prod)\n    ys[prod], Es[prod] = y, E\n    zs_all.append((y*np.sqrt(E)).ravel())\nzs_all = np.concatenate(zs_all)\nc = 1.4826*np.median(np.abs(zs_all-np.median(zs_all)))\n# AR(1) on standardized residuals (clip extremes to limit dip influence)\nrho_n, rho_d = 0.0, 0.0\nfor prod in products:\n    z = ys[prod]*np.sqrt(Es[prod])/c\n    z = np.clip(z, -3, 3)\n    rho_n += (z[:, 1:]*z[:, :-1]).sum(); rho_d += (z[:, :-1]**2).sum()\nrho = rho_n/rho_d\nprint('noise c =', round(c, 3), ' AR1 rho =', round(rho, 3))\n\nresults = []\nfor prod in products:\n    y, E = ys[prod], Es[prod]\n    if NEG:\n        y = -y\n    w = np.sqrt(E)/c\n    yw = y*w\n    # AR(1) prewhiten along time within each store\n    yw2 = yw.copy(); yw2[:, 1:] -= rho*yw[:, :-1]\n    ywf = yw2.ravel()\n\n    keys, cols = [], []\n    for (pp, pr, whd), sl in batch_sales.items():\n        if pp != prod: continue\n        grid = {}\n        for s, d, q in sl:\n            f = q/E[s, d-1]\n            for li, k in enumerate(kernel):\n                t = d+1+li\n                if t > T: break\n                grid[(s, t-1)] = grid.get((s, t-1), 0.) + k*f*w[s, t-1]\n        if not grid: continue\n        # prewhiten exposure same way\n        g2 = {}\n        for (s, ti), v in grid.items():\n            g2[(s, ti)] = g2.get((s, ti), 0.) + v\n            if ti+1 < T:\n                g2[(s, ti+1)] = g2.get((s, ti+1), 0.) - rho*v\n        idx = np.array([s*T+ti for (s, ti) in g2])\n        val = np.array(list(g2.values()))\n        o = np.argsort(idx)\n        keys.append((pr, whd)); cols.append((idx[o], val[o]))\n    norms = np.array([np.sqrt((v**2).sum()) for _, v in cols])\n\n    r = ywf.copy(); active = []\n    for it in range(60):\n        zsc = np.array([(r[i]*v).sum() for i, v in cols])/norms\n        for a in active: zsc[a] = -np.inf\n        j = int(np.argmax(zsc))\n        if zsc[j] < 4.5: break\n        active.append(j)\n        A = np.zeros((len(ywf), len(active)))\n        for ci, a in enumerate(active):\n            i, v = cols[a]; A[i, ci] = v\n        coef, _ = nnls(A, ywf)\n        r = ywf - A@coef\n    if active:\n        A = np.zeros((len(ywf), len(active)))\n        for ci, a in enumerate(active):\n            i, v = cols[a]; A[i, ci] = v\n        coef, _ = nnls(A, ywf)\n        ssef = ((ywf-A@coef)**2).sum()\n        for ci, a in enumerate(active):\n            if coef[ci] <= 0: continue\n            keep = [k for k in range(len(active)) if k != ci]\n            if keep:\n                c2, _ = nnls(A[:, keep], ywf)\n                sser = ((ywf-A[:, keep]@c2)**2).sum()\n            else:\n                sser = (ywf**2).sum()\n            results.append((prod, keys[a][0], keys[a][1], coef[ci],\n                            np.sqrt(max(sser-ssef, 0))))\n    print(prod, len([x for x in results if x[0] == prod]), flush=True)\n\nout = pd.DataFrame(results, columns=['product', 'producer', 'whd', 'coef', 'dropz'])\nfn = 'omp2_neg.pkl' if NEG else 'omp2_pos.pkl'\nout.to_pickle(fn)\nprint('saved', fn, len(out))\nprint(out.sort_values('dropz', ascending=False).head(40).to_string())\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null,"userModified":false}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-fable-5","id":"msg_012ijB7e6zDCmMTNgFPAmCZS","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01SuaudaVYmmNeoumWKCb7DL","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/app/confirm.py","content":"\"\"\"Knockout confirmation: producer X is confirmed for product p iff the\ndemand trajectory cannot be explained without X causing >=1 rejection.\nFast sparse OMP with NNLS refits.\"\"\"\nimport pandas as pd, numpy as np, pickle, sys, json\nfrom collections import defaultdict\nfrom scipy.optimize import nnls\nimport scipy.sparse as sp\n\nwith open('consumed.pkl','rb') as f: consumed = pickle.load(f)\nds = pd.read_pickle('day_sales.pkl')\npiv = ds.pivot_table(index='Day', columns=['Product_ID','Store_ID'], values='demand', fill_value=0)\nproducts = sorted(piv.columns.levels[0], key=lambda s:int(s.split('_')[1]))\nstores = sorted(piv[products[0]].columns, key=lambda s:int(s.split('_')[1]))\nst_idx = {s:i for i,s in enumerate(stores)}\nT = 365\nkernel = np.array([0.8]*3 + [0.5]*4 + [0.2]*7)\nENTRY_Z = 4.5\n\nbatch_sales = defaultdict(list)\nfor (day, st, prod), lots in consumed.items():\n    for pr, qty, whd in lots:\n        batch_sales[(prod, pr, whd)].append((st_idx[st], day, qty))\n\ndef rolling_median_masked(x, mask, win=41):\n    n = len(x); h = win//2\n    out = np.empty(n)\n    for i in range(n):\n        lo, hi = max(0, i-h), min(n, i+h+1)\n        seg = x[lo:hi][~mask[lo:hi]]\n        out[i] = np.median(seg) if len(seg) >= 8 else np.nan\n    return pd.Series(out).ffill().bfill().values\n\ndef build_y(prod):\n    D = piv[prod][stores].values.astype(float)\n    base = np.median(D, axis=0)\n    cal = np.median(D/base, axis=1)\n    E = np.empty_like(D)\n    for s in range(10):\n        r0 = D[:, s]/cal\n        mask = np.zeros(T, bool)\n        for _ in range(3):\n            L = rolling_median_masked(r0, mask)\n            e = cal*L\n            yv = 1 - D[:, s]/e\n            mask = yv > 2.5*(0.34/np.sqrt(e))\n        E[:, s] = cal*L\n    return (1 - D/E).T, E.T\n\nys, Es = {}, {}\nzs_all = []\nfor prod in products:\n    y, E = build_y(prod)\n    ys[prod], Es[prod] = y, E\n    zs_all.append((y*np.sqrt(E)).ravel())\nzs_all = np.concatenate(zs_all)\nc = 1.4826*np.median(np.abs(zs_all-np.median(zs_all)))\nrho_n = rho_d = 0.0\nfor prod in products:\n    z = np.clip(ys[prod]*np.sqrt(Es[prod])/c, -3, 3)\n    rho_n += (z[:,1:]*z[:,:-1]).sum(); rho_d += (z[:,:-1]**2).sum()\nrho = rho_n/rho_d\nprint('c=', round(c,3), 'rho=', round(rho,3))\n\ndef greedy(Bc, norms, yw, allowed, entry=ENTRY_Z, max_iter=80):\n    \"\"\"sparse OMP with NNLS refit; returns active idx, coefs, sse\"\"\"\n    r = yw.copy(); active = []\n    Adense = None\n    for _ in range(max_iter):\n        zsc = (Bc.T @ r)/norms\n        zsc[~allowed] = -np.inf\n        if active: zsc[active] = -np.inf\n        j = int(np.argmax(zsc))\n        if zsc[j] < entry: break\n        active.append(j)\n        col = Bc[:, j].toarray().ravel()\n        Adense = col[:,None] if Adense is None else np.column_stack([Adense, col])\n        coef, _ = nnls(Adense, yw)\n        r = yw - Adense@coef\n    if not active:\n        return [], np.zeros(0), (yw**2).sum()\n    coef, _ = nnls(Adense, yw)\n    sse = ((yw - Adense@coef)**2).sum()\n    return active, coef, sse\n\nconfirm_rows = []\ndetail = {}\nfor prod in products:\n    y, E = ys[prod], Es[prod]\n    w = np.sqrt(E)/c\n    yw = y*w\n    yw2 = yw.copy(); yw2[:,1:] -= rho*yw[:,:-1]\n    ywf = yw2.ravel()\n\n    keys = []; data=[]; rows=[]; colidx=[]\n    for (pp, pr, whd), sl in batch_sales.items():\n        if pp != prod: continue\n        grid = {}\n        for s, d, q in sl:\n            f = q/E[s, d-1]\n            for li, k in enumerate(kernel):\n                t = d+1+li\n                if t > T: break\n                grid[(s,t-1)] = grid.get((s,t-1),0.) + k*f*w[s,t-1]\n        if not grid: continue\n        g2 = {}\n        for (s,ti), v in grid.items():\n            g2[(s,ti)] = g2.get((s,ti),0.) + v\n            if ti+1 < T: g2[(s,ti+1)] = g2.get((s,ti+1),0.) - rho*v\n        ci = len(keys); keys.append((pr,whd))\n        for (s,ti), v in g2.items():\n            rows.append(s*T+ti); colidx.append(ci); data.append(v)\n    nb = len(keys)\n    Bc = sp.csc_matrix((data,(rows,colidx)), shape=(10*T, nb))\n    norms = np.sqrt(Bc.multiply(Bc).sum(axis=0)).A1\n    norms[norms==0] = 1\n\n    allowed_all = np.ones(nb, bool)\n    active, coef, sse_full = greedy(Bc, norms, ywf, allowed_all)\n    sel_producers = sorted({keys[a][0] for ai,a in enumerate(active) if coef[ai] > 0},\n                           key=lambda s:int(s.split('_')[1]))\n    prod_of_col = np.array([int(k[0].split('_')[1]) for k in keys])\n    det = []\n    for X in sel_producers:\n        xi = int(X.split('_')[1])\n        allowed = prod_of_col != xi\n        _,_, sse_r = greedy(Bc, norms, ywf, allowed)\n        zX = np.sqrt(max(sse_r - sse_full, 0))\n        batches = [(keys[a][1], round(float(coef[ai]),2))\n                   for ai,a in enumerate(active) if keys[a][0]==X and coef[ai]>0]\n        confirm_rows.append((prod, X, zX, len(batches)))\n        det.append((X, round(zX,1), batches))\n    detail[prod] = det\n    print(prod, det, flush=True)\n\ncf = pd.DataFrame(confirm_rows, columns=['product','producer','knockout_z','n_batches'])\ncf.to_pickle('knockout.pkl')\nwith open('knockout_detail.pkl','wb') as f: pickle.dump(detail, f)\nprint(cf.sort_values('knockout_z', ascending=False).to_string())\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":539,"cache_read_input_tokens":95235,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":539,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":36,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"9777808e-4b15-489a-a904-14dbfb0b648f","uuid":"a151f88a-cca2-4324-bb8d-12eea163e477","request_id":"req_011CbwP4ZuzvoNaZyLGnqu78"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-fable-5","id":"msg_01GbwATYNgs7SySAAwrA28xW","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01HoxgfpmTxjrbaLoomqdBYh","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/app/deconv.py","content":"\"\"\"Per-(store,product) nonnegative deconvolution of demand deficit onto the\nstaged recovery kernel. Impulses only on days with sales. Drop-test z per\nevent pins the day. Negative control via --neg.\"\"\"\nimport pandas as pd, numpy as np, pickle, sys\nfrom collections import defaultdict\nfrom scipy.optimize import nnls\n\nNEG = '--neg' in sys.argv\n\nwith open('consumed.pkl','rb') as f: consumed = pickle.load(f)\nds = pd.read_pickle('day_sales.pkl')\npiv = ds.pivot_table(index='Day', columns=['Product_ID','Store_ID'], values='demand', fill_value=0)\nproducts = sorted(piv.columns.levels[0], key=lambda s:int(s.split('_')[1]))\nstores = sorted(piv[products[0]].columns, key=lambda s:int(s.split('_')[1]))\nT = 365\nkernel = np.array([0.8]*3 + [0.5]*4 + [0.2]*7)   # lags 1..14\n\nsold_units = defaultdict(int)   # (s_name, prod, day) -> units sold\nfor (day, st, prod), lots in consumed.items():\n    sold_units[(st, prod, day)] = sum(q for _, q, _ in lots)\n\ndef rolling_median_masked(x, mask, win=41):\n    n = len(x); h = win//2\n    out = np.empty(n)\n    for i in range(n):\n        lo, hi = max(0, i-h), min(n, i+h+1)\n        seg = x[lo:hi][~mask[lo:hi]]\n        out[i] = np.median(seg) if len(seg) >= 8 else np.nan\n    return pd.Series(out).ffill().bfill().values\n\ndef build_y(prod):\n    D = piv[prod][stores].values.astype(float)\n    base = np.median(D, axis=0)\n    cal = np.median(D/base, axis=1)\n    E = np.empty_like(D)\n    for s in range(10):\n        r0 = D[:, s]/cal\n        mask = np.zeros(T, bool)\n        for _ in range(3):\n            L = rolling_median_masked(r0, mask)\n            e = cal*L\n            yv = 1 - D[:, s]/e\n            mask = yv > 2.5*(0.34/np.sqrt(e))\n        E[:, s] = cal*L\n    return (1 - D/E).T, E.T   # (10,365)\n\nys, Es = {}, {}\nzs_all = []\nfor prod in products:\n    y, E = build_y(prod)\n    ys[prod], Es[prod] = y, E\n    zs_all.append((y*np.sqrt(E)).ravel())\nzs_all = np.concatenate(zs_all)\nc = 1.4826*np.median(np.abs(zs_all-np.median(zs_all)))\nrho_n = rho_d = 0.0\nfor prod in products:\n    z = np.clip(ys[prod]*np.sqrt(Es[prod])/c, -3, 3)\n    rho_n += (z[:,1:]*z[:,:-1]).sum(); rho_d += (z[:,:-1]**2).sum()\nrho = rho_n/rho_d\nprint('c =', round(c,3), 'rho =', round(rho,3))\n\nevents = []\nfor prod in products:\n    y, E = ys[prod], Es[prod]\n    if NEG: y = -y\n    for s, st in enumerate(stores):\n        w = np.sqrt(E[s])/c\n        yw = y[s]*w\n        yw2 = yw.copy(); yw2[1:] -= rho*yw[:-1]\n        # candidate impulse days: sold > 0\n        days = [t for t in range(1, T+1) if sold_units.get((st, prod, t), 0) > 0]\n        if not days: continue\n        # design: column per impulse day tau: kernel at tau+1..tau+14, whitened\n        A = np.zeros((T, len(days)))\n        for ci, tau in enumerate(days):\n            colv = np.zeros(T)\n            for li, k in enumerate(kernel):\n                t = tau+1+li\n                if t > T: break\n                colv[t-1] = k*w[t-1]\n            col2 = colv.copy(); col2[1:] -= rho*colv[:-1]\n            A[:, ci] = col2\n        coef, _ = nnls(A, yw2)\n        resid = yw2 - A@coef\n        sse_full = (resid**2).sum()\n        nz = np.where(coef > 1e-9)[0]\n        if len(nz) == 0: continue\n        # drop-test each nonzero impulse (refit all others)\n        for ci in nz:\n            keep = [k for k in range(len(days)) if k != ci]\n            c2, _ = nnls(A[:, keep], yw2)\n            sse_r = ((yw2 - A[:, keep]@c2)**2).sum()\n            zt = np.sqrt(max(sse_r - sse_full, 0))\n            if zt >= 3.0:\n                events.append((prod, st, days[ci], float(coef[ci]), float(zt)))\n    print(prod, len([e for e in events if e[0]==prod]), flush=True)\n\nev = pd.DataFrame(events, columns=['product','store','day','amp','z'])\nfn = 'events_neg.pkl' if NEG else 'events_pos.pkl'\nev.to_pickle(fn)\nprint('saved', fn, len(ev))\nprint('z quantiles:', np.quantile(ev.z, [0.5,0.9,0.99,1.0]).round(2) if len(ev) else '-')\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1433,"cache_read_input_tokens":103901,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":1433,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":46,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"9777808e-4b15-489a-a904-14dbfb0b648f","uuid":"a7223cbd-e10d-46ff-9e72-5266eaaf9f32","request_id":"req_011CbwPDPtFZn3N1Qg4gpSAu"}

Impulses only on days with sales. Drop-test z per\nevent pins the day. Negative control via --neg.\"\"\"\nimport pandas as pd, numpy as np, pickle, sys\nfrom collections import defaultdict\nfrom scipy.optimize import nnls\n\nNEG = '--neg' in sys.argv\n\nwith open('consumed.pkl','rb') as f: consumed = pickle.load(f)\nds = pd.read_pickle('day_sales.pkl')\npiv = ds.pivot_table(index='Day', columns=['Product_ID','Store_ID'], values='demand', fill_value=0)\nproducts = sorted(piv.columns.levels[0], key=lambda s:int(s.split('_')[1]))\nstores = sorted(piv[products[0]].columns, key=lambda s:int(s.split('_')[1]))\nT = 365\nkernel = np.array([0.8]*3 + [0.5]*4 + [0.2]*7)   # lags 1..14\n\nsold_units = defaultdict(int)   # (s_name, prod, day) -> units sold\nfor (day, st, prod), lots in consumed.items():\n    sold_units[(st, prod, day)] = sum(q for _, q, _ in lots)\n\ndef rolling_median_masked(x, mask, win=41):\n    n = len(x); h = win//2\n    out = np.empty(n)\n    for i in range(n):\n        lo, hi = max(0, i-h), min(n, i+h+1)\n        seg = x[lo:hi][~mask[lo:hi]]\n        out[i] = np.median(seg) if len(seg) >= 8 else np.nan\n    return pd.Series(out).ffill().bfill().values\n\ndef build_y(prod):\n    D = piv[prod][stores].values.astype(float)\n    base = np.median(D, axis=0)\n    cal = np.median(D/base, axis=1)\n    E = np.empty_like(D)\n    for s in range(10):\n        r0 = D[:, s]/cal\n        mask = np.zeros(T, bool)\n        for _ in range(3):\n            L = rolling_median_masked(r0, mask)\n            e = cal*L\n            yv = 1 - D[:, s]/e\n            mask = yv > 2.5*(0.34/np.sqrt(e))\n        E[:, s] = cal*L\n    return (1 - D/E).T, E.T   # (10,365)\n\nys, Es = {}, {}\nzs_all = []\nfor prod in products:\n    y, E = build_y(prod)\n    ys[prod], Es[prod] = y, E\n    zs_all.append((y*np.sqrt(E)).ravel())\nzs_all = np.concatenate(zs_all)\nc = 1.4826*np.median(np.abs(zs_all-np.median(zs_all)))\nrho_n = rho_d = 0.0\nfor prod in products:\n    z = np.clip(ys[prod]*np.sqrt(Es[prod])/c, -3, 3)\n    rho_n += (z[:,1:]*z[:,:-1]).sum(); rho_d += (z[:,:-1]**2).sum()\nrho = rho_n/rho_d\nprint('c =', round(c,3), 'rho =', round(rho,3))\n\nevents = []\nfor prod in products:\n    y, E = ys[prod], Es[prod]\n    if NEG: y = -y\n    for s, st in enumerate(stores):\n        w = np.sqrt(E[s])/c\n        yw = y[s]*w\n        yw2 = yw.copy(); yw2[1:] -= rho*yw[:-1]\n        # candidate impulse days: sold > 0\n        days = [t for t in range(1, T+1) if sold_units.get((st, prod, t), 0) > 0]\n        if not days: continue\n        # design: column per impulse day tau: kernel at tau+1..tau+14, whitened\n        A = np.zeros((T, len(days)))\n        for ci, tau in enumerate(days):\n            colv = np.zeros(T)\n            for li, k in enumerate(kernel):\n                t = tau+1+li\n                if t > T: break\n                colv[t-1] = k*w[t-1]\n            col2 = colv.copy(); col2[1:] -= rho*colv[:-1]\n            A[:, ci] = col2\n        coef, _ = nnls(A, yw2)\n        resid = yw2 - A@coef\n        sse_full = (resid**2).sum()\n        nz = np.where(coef > 1e-9)[0]\n        if len(nz) == 0: continue\n        # drop-test each nonzero impulse (refit all others)\n        for ci in nz:\n            keep = [k for k in range(len(days)) if k != ci]\n            c2, _ = nnls(A[:, keep], yw2)\n            sse_r = ((yw2 - A[:, keep]@c2)**2).sum()\n            zt = np.sqrt(max(sse_r - sse_full, 0))\n            if zt >= 3.0:\n                events.append((prod, st, days[ci], float(coef[ci]), float(zt)))\n    print(prod, len([e for e in events if e[0]==prod]), flush=True)\n\nev = pd.DataFrame(events, columns=['product','store','day','amp','z'])\nfn = 'events_neg.pkl' if NEG else 'events_pos.pkl'\nev.to_pickle(fn)\nprint('saved', fn, len(ev))\nprint('z quantiles:', np.quantile(ev.z, [0.5,0.9,0.99,1.0]).round(2) if len(ev) else '-')\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null,"userModified":false}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-fable-5","id":"msg_01P3hLtRHb85FixR4LHNCZF9","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_019Mra4KrhLM5d54vXkRBTtq","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"python3 << 'EOF'\nimport pandas as pd, numpy as np\nsales = pd.read_csv('sales_log.csv')\nsub = sales[(sales.Store_ID=='S_3')&(sales.Product_ID=='P_7')]\n# positional correlation between consecutive days with equal row counts\nby_day = {d: g[['Reported_Demand','Sold_Quantity','Lost_Sales']].values for d,g in sub.groupby('Day')}\ncors, sh = [], []\nrng = np.random.default_rng(0)\nfor d in range(1,365):\n    a = by_day.get(d); b = by_day.get(d+1)\n    if a is None or b is None or len(a)!=len(b) or len(a)<6: continue\n    x, y = a[:,0].astype(float), b[:,0].astype(float)\n    if x.std()==0 or y.std()==0: continue\n    cors.append(np.corrcoef(x,y)[0,1])\n    sh.append(np.corrcoef(x, rng.permutation(y))[0,1])\nprint('same-position corr:', np.mean(cors).round(3), 'n=',len(cors), ' shuffled:', np.mean(sh).round(3))\n\n# Also: is Sold_Quantity allocated in row order? check a day with partial stockout:\n# find day where lost>0 for some rows and sold>0 for others\nd = sub[(sub.Day==168)]\nprint('\\nS_3 P_7 day 168 rows (demand, sold, lost):')\nprint(d[['Reported_Demand','Sold_Quantity','Lost_Sales']].to_string(index=False))\nd2 = sub[(sub.Day==179)]\nprint('\\nday 179 rows:')\nprint(d2[['Reported_Demand','Sold_Quantity','Lost_Sales']].to_string(index=False))\nEOF","description":"Test consumer row-order stability and serving order"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":7844,"cache_read_input_tokens":111525,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":7844,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":40,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"9777808e-4b15-489a-a904-14dbfb0b648f","uuid":"b8e218ba-937d-4dd7-b15d-f9fb04713b7d","request_id":"req_011CbwPUWLrhgx3TRf88MSBT"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-fable-5","id":"msg_013CH9gxYMLUmyPNmkdy6M9T","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01U2kkMmsbu8tjeKXvMiDx71","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/app/bounded.py","content":"\"\"\"Bounded attribution with exact per-lot consumer caps.\n\nFor each lot sold at (store,product,day): cap = sum of Reported_Demand of the\nconsumer rows (serving order) whose purchased unit range intersects the lot.\nDeficit (units) <= sum over bad lots of kernel * cap * coef, 0<=coef<=UB.\nGreedy entry + bounded refit (lsq_linear). Knockout per producer ->\nz_X = sqrt(SSE_without_X - SSE_full). Negative control calibrates the null.\n\"\"\"\nimport pandas as pd, numpy as np, pickle, sys\nfrom collections import defaultdict\nfrom scipy.optimize import lsq_linear\nimport scipy.sparse as sp\n\nUB = 1.25\nENTRY = 4.0\n\nwith open('consumed.pkl','rb') as f: consumed = pickle.load(f)\nsales = pd.read_csv('sales_log.csv')\nds = pd.read_pickle('day_sales.pkl')\npiv = ds.pivot_table(index='Day', columns=['Product_ID','Store_ID'], values='demand', fill_value=0)\nproducts = sorted(piv.columns.levels[0], key=lambda s:int(s.split('_')[1]))\nstores = sorted(piv[products[0]].columns, key=lambda s:int(s.split('_')[1]))\nst_idx = {s:i for i,s in enumerate(stores)}\nT = 365\nkernel = np.array([0.8]*3 + [0.5]*4 + [0.2]*7)\n\n# ---- caps: lot -> consumer-demand mass per sale day -------------------------\nprint('computing lot caps...', flush=True)\nsales_sorted = sales.sort_index()\ngrp = sales_sorted.groupby(['Day','Store_ID','Product_ID'], sort=False)\nlot_caps = defaultdict(list)   # (prod, producer, whd) -> [(s_idx, day, cap)]\nfor (day, st, prod), g in grp:\n    lots = consumed.get((day, st, prod))\n    if not lots: continue\n    dem = g.Reported_Demand.values; sold = g.Sold_Quantity.values\n    cum = np.cumsum(sold)\n    row_iv = [(cum[i]-sold[i], cum[i]) for i in range(len(sold)) if sold[i] > 0]\n    row_dem = [dem[i] for i in range(len(sold)) if sold[i] > 0]\n    pos = 0\n    for pr, q, whd in lots:\n        lo, hi = pos, pos+q\n        cap = sum(d for (a,b), d in zip(row_iv, row_dem) if a < hi and lo < b)\n        lot_caps[(prod, pr, whd)].append((st_idx[st], day, cap))\n        pos = hi\nprint('lots:', len(lot_caps), flush=True)\n\n# ---- demand model (as before) ----------------------------------------------\ndef rolling_median_masked(x, mask, win=41):\n    n = len(x); h = win//2\n    out = np.empty(n)\n    for i in range(n):\n        lo, hi = max(0, i-h), min(n, i+h+1)\n        seg = x[lo:hi][~mask[lo:hi]]\n        out[i] = np.median(seg) if len(seg) >= 8 else np.nan\n    return pd.Series(out).ffill().bfill().values\n\ndef build_y(prod):\n    D = piv[prod][stores].values.astype(float)\n    base = np.median(D, axis=0)\n    cal = np.median(D/base, axis=1)\n    E = np.empty_like(D)\n    for s in range(10):\n        r0 = D[:, s]/cal\n        mask = np.zeros(T, bool)\n        for _ in range(3):\n            L = rolling_median_masked(r0, mask)\n            yv = 1 - D[:, s]/(cal*L)\n            mask = yv > 2.5*(0.34/np.sqrt(cal*L))\n        E[:, s] = cal*L\n    return (1 - D/E).T, E.T\n\nys, Es, zs_all = {}, {}, []\nfor prod in products:\n    y, E = build_y(prod)\n    ys[prod], Es[prod] = y, E\n    zs_all.append((y*np.sqrt(E)).ravel())\nzs_all = np.concatenate(zs_all)\nc = 1.4826*np.median(np.abs(zs_all-np.median(zs_all)))\nrho_n = rho_d = 0.0\nfor prod in products:\n    z = np.clip(ys[prod]*np.sqrt(Es[prod])/c, -3, 3)\n    rho_n += (z[:,1:]*z[:,:-1]).sum(); rho_d += (z[:,:-1]**2).sum()\nrho = rho_n/rho_d\nprint('c=',round(c,3),'rho=',round(rho,3), flush=True)\n\ndef greedy_bounded(Bc, norms, yw, allowed, entry=ENTRY, max_iter=120):\n    r = yw.copy(); active=[]; A=None\n    for _ in range(max_iter):\n        zsc = (Bc.T @ r)/norms\n        zsc[~allowed] = -np.inf\n        if active: zsc[active] = -np.inf\n        j = int(np.argmax(zsc))\n        if zsc[j] < entry: break\n        active.append(j)\n        col = Bc[:, j].toarray().ravel()\n        A = col[:,None] if A is None else np.column_stack([A, col])\n        res = lsq_linear(A, yw, bounds=(0, UB), tol=1e-8)\n        r = yw - A@res.x\n    if not active:\n        return [], np.zeros(0), (yw**2).sum()\n    res = lsq_linear(A, yw, bounds=(0, UB), tol=1e-8)\n    sse = ((yw - A@res.x)**2).sum()\n    return active, res.x, sse\n\nrun_results = {}\nfor MODE in ('pos','neg'):\n    all_rows = []\n    for prod in products:\n        y, E = ys[prod], Es[prod]\n        if MODE=='neg': y = -y\n        w = np.sqrt(E)/c\n        yw = y*E/(c*np.sqrt(E))          # whitened unit-deficit == y*sqrt(E)/c\n        yw2 = yw.copy(); yw2[:,1:] -= rho*yw[:,:-1]\n        ywf = yw2.ravel()\n\n        keys=[]; data=[]; rows=[]; cidx=[]\n        for (pp, pr, whd), sl in lot_caps.items():\n            if pp != prod: continue\n            grid = {}\n            for s, d, cap in sl:\n                if cap <= 0: continue\n                for li,k in enumerate(kernel):\n                    t = d+1+li\n                    if t > T: break\n                    grid[(s,t-1)] = grid.get((s,t-1),0.) + k*cap/(c*np.sqrt(E[s,t-1]))\n            if not grid: continue\n            g2 = {}\n            for (s,ti),v in grid.items():\n                g2[(s,ti)] = g2.get((s,ti),0.) + v\n                if ti+1 < T: g2[(s,ti+1)] = g2.get((s,ti+1),0.) - rho*v\n            ci = len(keys); keys.append((pr,whd))\n            for (s,ti),v in g2.items():\n                rows.append(s*T+ti); cidx.append(ci); data.append(v)\n        nb = len(keys)\n        Bc = sp.csc_matrix((data,(rows,cidx)), shape=(10*T, nb))\n        norms = np.sqrt(Bc.multiply(Bc).sum(axis=0)).A1; norms[norms==0]=1\n\n        allowed = np.ones(nb, bool)\n        active, coef, sse_full = greedy_bounded(Bc, norms, ywf, allowed)\n        sel_pr = sorted({keys[a][0] for ai,a in enumerate(active) if coef[ai] > 0.05},\n                        key=lambda s:int(s.split('_')[1]))\n        prnum = np.array([int(k[0].split('_')[1]) for k in keys])\n        for X in sel_pr:\n            xi = int(X.split('_')[1])\n            _,_, sse_r = greedy_bounded(Bc, norms, ywf, prnum != xi)\n            zX = np.sqrt(max(sse_r - sse_full, 0))\n            batches = [(keys[a][1], round(float(coef[ai]),2))\n                       for ai,a in enumerate(active) if keys[a][0]==X and coef[ai]>0.05]\n            all_rows.append((prod, X, zX, len(batches), batches))\n        print(MODE, prod, [(r[1], round(r[2],1)) for r in all_rows if r[0]==prod], flush=True)\n    run_results[MODE] = pd.DataFrame(all_rows,\n        columns=['product','producer','knockz','nb','batches'])\n\nwith open('bounded_results.pkl','wb') as f: pickle.dump(run_results, f)\npos, neg = run_results['pos'], run_results['neg']\nprint('\\nNEG knockz quantiles:', np.quantile(neg.knockz,[0.5,0.9,1.0]).round(2) if len(neg) else 'none')\nprint('POS knockz top:'); print(pos.sort_values('knockz',ascending=False).head(50).to_string())\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":4598,"cache_read_input_tokens":119369,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":4598,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":41,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"9777808e-4b15-489a-a904-14dbfb0b648f","uuid":"f8904ee7-f88e-42e8-aa8e-1814adf8b323","request_id":"req_011CbwPakJQyyMCE2PKkRWG9"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-fable-5","id":"msg_01SJqxZW9MEzGebFSWzuiXFv","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01NmyFD4QEzLtvH31sRDbDTc","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"python3 << 'EOF'\nimport pandas as pd, numpy as np, pickle\nfrom collections import defaultdict\n\nwith open('consumed.pkl','rb') as f: consumed = pickle.load(f)\nds = pd.read_pickle('day_sales.pkl')\npiv = ds.pivot_table(index='Day', columns=['Product_ID','Store_ID'], values='demand', fill_value=0)\nproducts = sorted(piv.columns.levels[0], key=lambda s:int(s.split('_')[1]))\nstores = sorted(piv[products[0]].columns, key=lambda s:int(s.split('_')[1]))\nst_idx = {s:i for i,s in enumerate(stores)}\nT=365; kernel = np.array([0.8]*3+[0.5]*4+[0.2]*7)\n\ndef rolling_median_masked(x, mask, win=41):\n    n=len(x); h=win//2; out=np.empty(n)\n    for i in range(n):\n        lo,hi = max(0,i-h),min(n,i+h+1)\n        seg = x[lo:hi][~mask[lo:hi]]\n        out[i] = np.median(seg) if len(seg)>=8 else np.nan\n    return pd.Series(out).ffill().bfill().values\n\ndef build_y(prod):\n    D = piv[prod][stores].values.astype(float)\n    base = np.median(D,axis=0); cal = np.median(D/base,axis=1)\n    E = np.empty_like(D)\n    for s in range(10):\n        r0 = D[:,s]/cal; mask=np.zeros(T,bool)\n        for _ in range(3):\n            L = rolling_median_masked(r0,mask)\n            yv = 1-D[:,s]/(cal*L)\n            mask = yv > 2.5*(0.34/np.sqrt(cal*L))\n        E[:,s] = cal*L\n    return (1-D/E).T, E.T\n\nbatch_sales = defaultdict(list)\nfor (day, st, prod), lots in consumed.items():\n    for pr,q,whd in lots:\n        batch_sales[(prod,pr,whd)].append((st_idx[st],day,q))\n\nC=0.305\nrows=[]\nfor prod in products:\n    y,E = build_y(prod)\n    w = np.sqrt(E)/C\n    for (pp,pr,whd),sl in batch_sales.items():\n        if pp!=prod: continue\n        num=den=0.0; units=0\n        for s,d,q in sl:\n            units+=q; f=q/E[s,d-1]\n            for li,k in enumerate(kernel):\n                t=d+1+li\n                if t>T: break\n                x=k*f*w[s,t-1]\n                num += y[s,t-1]*w[s,t-1]*x; den += x*x\n        if den>0: rows.append((prod,pr,whd,units,num/np.sqrt(den)))\nbs = pd.DataFrame(rows,columns=['product','producer','whd','units','z'])\nbs.to_pickle('batch_z2.pkl')\n\n# aggregate per (producer, product)\nagg = bs.groupby(['product','producer']).agg(n=('z','size'), meanz=('z','mean'),\n       poolz=('z', lambda v: v.sum()/np.sqrt(len(v))), hi=('z',lambda v:(v>3).sum())).reset_index()\nprint('top pooled (producer,product):')\nprint(agg.sort_values('poolz',ascending=False).head(25).to_string())\n# distribution of batch z for a top pair vs typical\nfor prod,pr in [('P_29','PR_60'),('P_17','PR_32'),('P_12','PR_7'),('P_7','PR_27'),('P_7','PR_50')]:\n    v = bs[(bs['product']==prod)&(bs.producer==pr)].z\n    print(f'\\n{prod} {pr}: n={len(v)} mean={v.mean():.2f} | z-hist:', \n          np.histogram(v, bins=[-9,-3,-1,1,3,5,7,20])[0].tolist())\nref = bs[(bs['product']=='P_5')].z\nprint('\\nALL P_5 batches (low-signal product): mean=%.2f'%ref.mean(), 'hist:',\n      np.histogram(ref, bins=[-9,-3,-1,1,3,5,7,20])[0].tolist())\nEOF","description":"Pool per-batch z by producer-product: chronic vs sporadic","timeout":300000},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":2705,"cache_read_input_tokens":135955,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":2705,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":51,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"9777808e-4b15-489a-a904-14dbfb0b648f","uuid":"8f6e3921-920e-49ac-9470-295eff2b5078","request_id":"req_011CbwPrNWSGx3ScYidLyUZT"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-fable-5","id":"msg_01GcBayahLNGBYieDuwKXEYd","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01XTbEAbPrM7R4JUPzeNh6QV","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/app/final_reg.py","content":"\"\"\"Joint producer-level regression per product with improved baseline,\nAR(1) prewhitening, and t-stats. Negative control on -y.\"\"\"\nimport pandas as pd, numpy as np, pickle\nfrom collections import defaultdict\n\nwith open('consumed.pkl','rb') as f: consumed = pickle.load(f)\nds = pd.read_pickle('day_sales.pkl')\npiv = ds.pivot_table(index='Day', columns=['Product_ID','Store_ID'], values='demand', fill_value=0)\nproducts = sorted(piv.columns.levels[0], key=lambda s:int(s.split('_')[1]))\nstores = sorted(piv[products[0]].columns, key=lambda s:int(s.split('_')[1]))\nst_idx = {s:i for i,s in enumerate(stores)}\nproducers = [f'PR_{i}' for i in range(1,61)]\npr_idx = {p:i for i,p in enumerate(producers)}\nT=365; kernel = np.array([0.8]*3+[0.5]*4+[0.2]*7)\n\ndef rolling_median_masked(x, mask, win=41):\n    n=len(x); h=win//2; out=np.empty(n)\n    for i in range(n):\n        lo,hi = max(0,i-h),min(n,i+h+1)\n        seg = x[lo:hi][~mask[lo:hi]]\n        out[i] = np.median(seg) if len(seg)>=8 else np.nan\n    return pd.Series(out).ffill().bfill().values\n\ndef build_y(prod):\n    D = piv[prod][stores].values.astype(float)\n    base = np.median(D,axis=0); cal = np.median(D/base,axis=1)\n    E = np.empty_like(D)\n    for s in range(10):\n        r0 = D[:,s]/cal; mask=np.zeros(T,bool)\n        for _ in range(3):\n            L = rolling_median_masked(r0,mask)\n            yv = 1-D[:,s]/(cal*L)\n            mask = yv > 2.5*(0.34/np.sqrt(cal*L))\n        E[:,s] = cal*L\n    return (1-D/E).T, E.T\n\nU = {p: np.zeros((10, T+1, 60)) for p in products}\nfor (day, st, prod), lots in consumed.items():\n    for pr,q,whd in lots:\n        U[prod][st_idx[st], day, pr_idx[pr]] += q\n\nC=0.305; RHO=0.384\nres_rows = []\nfor prod in products:\n    y,E = build_y(prod)\n    w = np.sqrt(E)/C\n    for sign, tag in [(1,'pos'), (-1,'neg')]:\n        yw = sign*y*w\n        yw2 = yw.copy(); yw2[:,1:] -= RHO*yw[:,:-1]\n        # exposure design\n        X = np.zeros((10, T, 60))\n        u = U[prod]\n        for s in range(10):\n            un = u[s,1:,:]/E[s][:,None]\n            for li,k in enumerate(kernel):\n                lag = 1+li\n                X[s,lag:,:] += k*un[:-lag]\n            X[s] *= w[s][:,None]\n        X2 = X.copy(); X2[:,1:,:] -= RHO*X[:,:-1,:]\n        Xf = X2.reshape(-1,60); yf = yw2.ravel()\n        mask = np.ones(10*T,bool)\n        for s in range(10): mask[s*T:s*T+15]=False\n        A = np.column_stack([np.ones(mask.sum()), Xf[mask]])\n        b = yf[mask]\n        coef, _, _, _ = np.linalg.lstsq(A,b,rcond=None)\n        r = b - A@coef\n        s2 = (r**2).sum()/(len(b)-A.shape[1])\n        cov = s2*np.linalg.inv(A.T@A)\n        tstats = coef[1:]/np.sqrt(np.diag(cov)[1:])\n        for j in range(60):\n            res_rows.append((tag, prod, producers[j], coef[1+j], tstats[j]))\n    print(prod, flush=True)\n\nrr = pd.DataFrame(res_rows, columns=['tag','product','producer','beta','t'])\nrr.to_pickle('final_reg.pkl')\npos = rr[rr.tag=='pos']; neg = rr[rr.tag=='neg']\nprint('NEG t quantiles:', np.quantile(neg.t,[0.5,0.99,0.999,1.0]).round(2))\nprint('POS t quantiles:', np.quantile(pos.t,[0.5,0.99,0.999,1.0]).round(2))\nprint('\\nPOS pairs with t>5:', (pos.t>5).sum(), ' NEG pairs with t>5:', (neg.t>5).sum())\nprint('\\ntop POS by t:')\nprint(pos.sort_values('t',ascending=False).head(60).to_string())\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":2545,"cache_read_input_tokens":144293,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":2545,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":76,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"9777808e-4b15-489a-a904-14dbfb0b648f","uuid":"427e17ef-ca03-4572-bdbf-4fc36a9f23f9","request_id":"req_011CbwPzqRubsevom2cCZRfr"}

Negative control on -y.\"\"\"\nimport pandas as pd, numpy as np, pickle\nfrom collections import defaultdict\n\nwith open('consumed.pkl','rb') as f: consumed = pickle.load(f)\nds = pd.read_pickle('day_sales.pkl')\npiv = ds.pivot_table(index='Day', columns=['Product_ID','Store_ID'], values='demand', fill_value=0)\nproducts = sorted(piv.columns.levels[0], key=lambda s:int(s.split('_')[1]))\nstores = sorted(piv[products[0]].columns, key=lambda s:int(s.split('_')[1]))\nst_idx = {s:i for i,s in enumerate(stores)}\nproducers = [f'PR_{i}' for i in range(1,61)]\npr_idx = {p:i for i,p in enumerate(producers)}\nT=365; kernel = np.array([0.8]*3+[0.5]*4+[0.2]*7)\n\ndef rolling_median_masked(x, mask, win=41):\n    n=len(x); h=win//2; out=np.empty(n)\n    for i in range(n):\n        lo,hi = max(0,i-h),min(n,i+h+1)\n        seg = x[lo:hi][~mask[lo:hi]]\n        out[i] = np.median(seg) if len(seg)>=8 else np.nan\n    return pd.Series(out).ffill().bfill().values\n\ndef build_y(prod):\n    D = piv[prod][stores].values.astype(float)\n    base = np.median(D,axis=0); cal = np.median(D/base,axis=1)\n    E = np.empty_like(D)\n    for s in range(10):\n        r0 = D[:,s]/cal; mask=np.zeros(T,bool)\n        for _ in range(3):\n            L = rolling_median_masked(r0,mask)\n            yv = 1-D[:,s]/(cal*L)\n            mask = yv > 2.5*(0.34/np.sqrt(cal*L))\n        E[:,s] = cal*L\n    return (1-D/E).T, E.T\n\nU = {p: np.zeros((10, T+1, 60)) for p in products}\nfor (day, st, prod), lots in consumed.items():\n    for pr,q,whd in lots:\n        U[prod][st_idx[st], day, pr_idx[pr]] += q\n\nC=0.305; RHO=0.384\nres_rows = []\nfor prod in products:\n    y,E = build_y(prod)\n    w = np.sqrt(E)/C\n    for sign, tag in [(1,'pos'), (-1,'neg')]:\n        yw = sign*y*w\n        yw2 = yw.copy(); yw2[:,1:] -= RHO*yw[:,:-1]\n        # exposure design\n        X = np.zeros((10, T, 60))\n        u = U[prod]\n        for s in range(10):\n            un = u[s,1:,:]/E[s][:,None]\n            for li,k in enumerate(kernel):\n                lag = 1+li\n                X[s,lag:,:] += k*un[:-lag]\n            X[s] *= w[s][:,None]\n        X2 = X.copy(); X2[:,1:,:] -= RHO*X[:,:-1,:]\n        Xf = X2.reshape(-1,60); yf = yw2.ravel()\n        mask = np.ones(10*T,bool)\n        for s in range(10): mask[s*T:s*T+15]=False\n        A = np.column_stack([np.ones(mask.sum()), Xf[mask]])\n        b = yf[mask]\n        coef, _, _, _ = np.linalg.lstsq(A,b,rcond=None)\n        r = b - A@coef\n        s2 = (r**2).sum()/(len(b)-A.shape[1])\n        cov = s2*np.linalg.inv(A.T@A)\n        tstats = coef[1:]/np.sqrt(np.diag(cov)[1:])\n        for j in range(60):\n            res_rows.append((tag, prod, producers[j], coef[1+j], tstats[j]))\n    print(prod, flush=True)\n\nrr = pd.DataFrame(res_rows, columns=['tag','product','producer','beta','t'])\nrr.to_pickle('final_reg.pkl')\npos = rr[rr.tag=='pos']; neg = rr[rr.tag=='neg']\nprint('NEG t quantiles:', np.quantile(neg.t,[0.5,0.99,0.999,1.0]).round(2))\nprint('POS t quantiles:', np.quantile(pos.t,[0.5,0.99,0.999,1.0]).round(2))\nprint('\\nPOS pairs with t>5:', (pos.t>5).sum(), ' NEG pairs with t>5:', (neg.t>5).sum())\nprint('\\ntop POS by t:')\nprint(pos.sort_values('t',ascending=False).head(60).to_string())\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":65,"oldLines":8,"newStart":65,"newLines":9,"lines":["         coef, _, _, _ = np.linalg.lstsq(A,b,rcond=None)","         r = b - A@coef","         s2 = (r**2).sum()/(len(b)-A.shape[1])","-        cov = s2*np.linalg.inv(A.T@A)","-        tstats = coef[1:]/np.sqrt(np.diag(cov)[1:])","+        cov = s2*np.linalg.pinv(A.T@A)","+        se = np.sqrt(np.maximum(np.diag(cov)[1:], 1e-12))","+        tstats = coef[1:]/se","         for j in range(60):","             res_rows.append((tag, prod, producers[j], coef[1+j], tstats[j]))","     print(prod, flush=True)"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}
