{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-fable-5","id":"msg_018KytdjSD5mnUYdkN9DriyK","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01KMojp6hYGuoes4aJiUoyPK","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/app/spillover_test.py","content":"\"\"\"For each candidate pair (X,p): recompute X's t using only cells NOT in the\nexposure window of OTHER strong producers (t>=6.5) of the same product.\"\"\"\nimport pandas as pd, numpy as np, pickle\nfrom collections import defaultdict\n\nwith open('consumed.pkl','rb') as f: consumed = pickle.load(f)\nds = pd.read_pickle('day_sales.pkl')\npiv = ds.pivot_table(index='Day', columns=['Product_ID','Store_ID'], values='demand', fill_value=0)\nproducts = sorted(piv.columns.levels[0], key=lambda s:int(s.split('_')[1]))\nstores = sorted(piv[products[0]].columns, key=lambda s:int(s.split('_')[1]))\nst_idx = {s:i for i,s in enumerate(stores)}\nproducers = [f'PR_{i}' for i in range(1,61)]\npr_idx = {p:i for i,p in enumerate(producers)}\nT=365; kernel = np.array([0.8]*3+[0.5]*4+[0.2]*7)\nC=0.305; RHO=0.384\n\ndef rolling_median_masked(x, mask, win=41):\n    n=len(x); h=win//2; out=np.empty(n)\n    for i in range(n):\n        lo,hi = max(0,i-h),min(n,i+h+1)\n        seg = x[lo:hi][~mask[lo:hi]]\n        out[i] = np.median(seg) if len(seg)>=8 else np.nan\n    return pd.Series(out).ffill().bfill().values\n\ndef build_y(prod):\n    D = piv[prod][stores].values.astype(float)\n    base = np.median(D,axis=0); cal = np.median(D/base,axis=1)\n    E = np.empty_like(D)\n    for s in range(10):\n        r0 = D[:,s]/cal; mask=np.zeros(T,bool)\n        for _ in range(3):\n            L = rolling_median_masked(r0,mask)\n            yv = 1-D[:,s]/(cal*L)\n            mask = yv > 2.5*(0.34/np.sqrt(cal*L))\n        E[:,s] = cal*L\n    return (1-D/E).T, E.T\n\nU = {p: np.zeros((10, T+1, 60)) for p in products}\nfor (day, st, prod), lots in consumed.items():\n    for pr,q,whd in lots:\n        U[prod][st_idx[st], day, pr_idx[pr]] += q\n\nrr = pd.read_pickle('final_reg.pkl')\npos = rr[rr.tag=='pos']\n\nout=[]\nfor prod in products:\n    sub = pos[(pos['product']==prod)&(pos.t>3.3)].sort_values('t',ascending=False)\n    if not len(sub): continue\n    strong = [r.producer for r in sub.itertuples() if r.t>=6.5]\n    y,E = build_y(prod)\n    w = np.sqrt(E)/C\n    yw = y*w; yw2 = yw.copy(); yw2[:,1:] -= RHO*yw[:,:-1]\n    u = U[prod]\n    X = np.zeros((10, T, 60))\n    for s in range(10):\n        un = u[s,1:,:]/E[s][:,None]\n        for li,k in enumerate(kernel):\n            X[s,1+li:,:] += k*un[:-(1+li)]\n        X[s] *= w[s][:,None]\n    X2 = X.copy(); X2[:,1:,:] -= RHO*X[:,:-1,:]\n    Xf = X2.reshape(-1,60); yf = yw2.ravel()\n    base_mask = np.ones(10*T,bool)\n    for s in range(10): base_mask[s*T:s*T+15]=False\n\n    for r in sub.itertuples():\n        Xn = r.producer\n        others = [pr_idx[q] for q in strong if q != Xn]\n        m = base_mask.copy()\n        if others:\n            expo = np.abs(X[:,:,others]).sum(axis=2).reshape(-1) > 1e-9\n            m &= ~expo\n        A = np.column_stack([np.ones(m.sum()), Xf[m]])\n        keep = A.std(axis=0) > 1e-12; keep[0]=True\n        A2 = A[:, keep]\n        b = yf[m]\n        coef,_,_,_ = np.linalg.lstsq(A2,b,rcond=None)\n        res = b - A2@coef\n        s2 = (res**2).sum()/(len(b)-A2.shape[1])\n        cov = s2*np.linalg.pinv(A2.T@A2)\n        # index of X among kept columns\n        full_idx = np.where(keep)[0]\n        ci = np.where(full_idx == 1+pr_idx[Xn])[0]\n        if len(ci)==0:\n            tc = np.nan\n        else:\n            ci = ci[0]\n            tc = coef[ci]/np.sqrt(max(cov[ci,ci],1e-12))\n        out.append((prod, Xn, r.t, tc, m.sum()))\n    print(prod, [(o[1], round(o[2],1), round(o[3],1)) for o in out if o[0]==prod], flush=True)\n\noo = pd.DataFrame(out, columns=['product','producer','t_orig','t_clean','ncells'])\noo.to_pickle('spill.pkl')\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":2530,"cache_read_input_tokens":153096,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":2530,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":74,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"9777808e-4b15-489a-a904-14dbfb0b648f","uuid":"acb4e937-9215-42be-bb26-7f0f046fc55a","request_id":"req_011CbwQCgm6ZCEdfMeNjqsSr"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-fable-5","id":"msg_0199GQpoi8TXAQ49vXqhjt54","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01Cq7Y1mwrwd4DC98oMjRyDY","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"python3 << 'EOF'\nimport pandas as pd, numpy as np, pickle\nfrom collections import defaultdict\n\nwith open('consumed.pkl','rb') as f: consumed = pickle.load(f)\nomp = pd.read_pickle('omp2_pos.pkl')   # improved-baseline OMP batches\nrr = pd.read_pickle('final_reg.pkl')\npos = rr[rr.tag=='pos']\n\n# sale cells per batch\nbatch_cells = defaultdict(list)  # (prod, producer, whd) -> [(store, day, qty)]\nfor (day, st, prod), lots in consumed.items():\n    for pr,q,whd in lots:\n        batch_cells[(prod,pr,whd)].append((st,day,q))\n\ncands = pos[pos.t>3.3]\nrows=[]\nfor prod in sorted(cands['product'].unique(), key=lambda s:int(s.split('_')[1])):\n    sub = cands[cands['product']==prod].sort_values('t',ascending=False)\n    prs = list(sub.producer)\n    ob = omp[omp['product']==prod]\n    # batch windows per producer (only OMP-selected batches = the events)\n    win = {}\n    for r in ob.itertuples():\n        cells = batch_cells[(prod, r.producer, r.whd)]\n        win.setdefault(r.producer, []).append((r.whd, r.dropz, cells))\n    for r in sub.itertuples():\n        X = r.producer\n        ev = win.get(X, [])\n        best_indep, best_any, n_ev = 0.0, 0.0, len(ev)\n        for whd, dz, cells in ev:\n            best_any = max(best_any, dz)\n            indep = True\n            for (st, d, q) in cells:\n                for Y, evs in win.items():\n                    if Y == X: continue\n                    tY = float(sub[sub.producer==Y].t.iloc[0]) if Y in prs else 0\n                    if tY < 6.5 and Y in prs: continue\n                    if Y not in prs: continue\n                    for whd2, dz2, cells2 in evs:\n                        for (st2, d2, q2) in cells2:\n                            if st2==st and abs(d2-d)<=14:\n                                indep=False\n            if indep: best_indep = max(best_indep, dz)\n        rows.append((prod, X, round(r.t,1), n_ev, round(best_any,1), round(best_indep,1)))\ndf = pd.DataFrame(rows, columns=['product','producer','t','n_batches','best_z','best_indep_z'])\nprint(df.to_string())\nEOF","description":"Event-independence analysis per candidate pair"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":131,"cache_creation_input_tokens":2747,"cache_read_input_tokens":160517,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":2747,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":34,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"9777808e-4b15-489a-a904-14dbfb0b648f","uuid":"474e268d-ca46-4b42-af98-6fda5610c3e4","request_id":"req_011CbwQJbH7a4dkqbggJ3sC9"}

defaultdict\n\nSEED = int(__import__('sys').argv[1]) if len(__import__('sys').argv)>1 else 1\nrng = np.random.default_rng(SEED)\nPREJ = 0.5\n\nwith open('consumed.pkl','rb') as f: consumed = pickle.load(f)\nds = pd.read_pickle('day_sales.pkl')\npiv = ds.pivot_table(index='Day', columns=['Product_ID','Store_ID'], values='demand', fill_value=0)\nproducts = sorted(piv.columns.levels[0], key=lambda s:int(s.split('_')[1]))\nstores = sorted(piv[products[0]].columns, key=lambda s:int(s.split('_')[1]))\nst_idx = {s:i for i,s in enumerate(stores)}\nproducers = [f'PR_{i}' for i in range(1,61)]\npr_idx = {p:i for i,p in enumerate(producers)}\nT=365; kernel = np.array([0.8]*3+[0.5]*4+[0.2]*7)\n\ndef rolling_median_masked(x, mask, win=41):\n    n=len(x); h=win//2; out=np.empty(n)\n    for i in range(n):\n        lo,hi = max(0,i-h),min(n,i+h+1)\n        seg = x[lo:hi][~mask[lo:hi]]\n        out[i] = np.median(seg) if len(seg)>=8 else np.nan\n    return pd.Series(out).ffill().bfill().values\n\ndef build_y_from(D):\n    base = np.median(D,axis=0); cal = np.median(D/base,axis=1)\n    E = np.empty_like(D)\n    for s in range(10):\n        r0 = D[:,s]/cal; mask=np.zeros(T,bool)\n        for _ in range(3):\n            L = rolling_median_masked(r0,mask)\n            yv = 1-D[:,s]/(cal*L)\n            mask = yv > 2.5*(0.34/np.sqrt(cal*L))\n        E[:,s] = cal*L\n    return (1-D/E).T, E.T\n\n# real expected demand per (s,p,t) for calibrating consumer lambdas\nE_real = {}\nfor prod in products:\n    D = piv[prod][stores].values.astype(float)\n    _, E = build_y_from(D)\n    E_real[prod] = E  # (10,365)\n\n# truth: bad pairs = final_reg t>=6.5\nrr = pd.read_pickle('final_reg.pkl')\npos = rr[rr.tag=='pos']\nbad_pairs = {(r['product'], r['producer']) for _, r in pos[pos.t>=6.5].iterrows()}\nprint('bad pairs:', len(bad_pairs))\n\n# lots per (prod, store, day) in FIFO order\nlots_by_cell = defaultdict(list)\nfor (day, st, prod), lots in consumed.items():\n    lots_by_cell[(prod, st_idx[st], day)] = lots\n\nLAM0 = 2.05\nmult_stage = np.array([1.0]+[0.2]*3+[0.5]*4+[0.8]*7)  # index=recovery day (0=normal)\n\nD_syn = {p: np.zeros((T,10)) for p in products}\nfor prod in products:\n    E = E_real[prod]\n    for s in range(10):\n        N = max(8, int(round(np.median(E[s])/LAM0)))\n        state = np.zeros(N, dtype=int)  # 0 normal else recovery day 1..14\n        for day in range(1, T+1):\n            lam_base = E[s, day-1]/N\n            mult = mult_stage[state]\n            lam = lam_base*mult\n            dem = rng.poisson(lam)\n            D_syn[prod][day-1, s] = dem.sum()\n            # serving\n            lots = lots_by_cell.get((prod, s, day), [])\n            avail = sum(q for _,q,_ in lots)\n            if avail > 0:\n                order = rng.permutation(N)\n                # lot unit boundaries\n                bounds = []\n                acc = 0\n                for pr,q,whd in lots:\n                    bounds.append((acc, acc+q, (prod,pr) in bad_pairs))\n                    acc += q\n                pos_u = 0\n                rejected = []\n                for ci in order:\n                    if dem[ci] == 0: continue\n                    take = min(dem[ci], avail - pos_u)\n                    if take <= 0: break\n                    a, b = pos_u, pos_u+take\n                    is_bad = any(lo < b and a < hi and bad for lo,hi,bad in bounds)\n                    if is_bad and rng.random() < PREJ:\n                        rejected.append(ci)\n                    pos_u = b\n                for ci in rejected:\n                    state[ci] = 1   # will become day1 (0.2) tomorrow via mult index\n            # advance recovery\n            adv = state > 0\n            state[adv] += 1\n            state[state > 14] = 0\n\n# pipeline on synthetic\nU = {p: np.zeros((10, T+1, 60)) for p in products}\nfor (day, st, prod), lots in consumed.items():\n    for pr,q,whd in lots:\n        U[prod][st_idx[st], day, pr_idx[pr]] += q\n\nys, Es, zs_all = {}, {}, []\nfor prod in products:\n    y,E = build_y_from(D_syn[prod])\n    ys[prod], Es[prod] = y,E\n    zs_all.append((y*np.sqrt(E)).ravel())\nzs_all = np.concatenate(zs_all)\nc = 1.4826*np.median(np.abs(zs_all-np.median(zs_all)))\nrho_n=rho_d=0.0\nfor prod in products:\n    z = np.clip(ys[prod]*np.sqrt(Es[prod])/c,-3,3)\n    rho_n += (z[:,1:]*z[:,:-1]).sum(); rho_d += (z[:,:-1]**2).sum()\nrho = rho_n/rho_d\nprint('syn c=',round(c,3),'rho=',round(rho,3))\n\nrows=[]\nfor prod in products:\n    y,E = ys[prod], Es[prod]\n    w = np.sqrt(E)/c\n    yw = y*w; yw2 = yw.copy(); yw2[:,1:] -= rho*yw[:,:-1]\n    X = np.zeros((10,T,60)); u = U[prod]\n    for s in range(10):\n        un = u[s,1:,:]/E[s][:,None]\n        for li,k in enumerate(kernel):\n            X[s,1+li:,:] += k*un[:-(1+li)]\n        X[s] *= w[s][:,None]\n    X2 = X.copy(); X2[:,1:,:] -= rho*X[:,:-1,:]\n    Xf = X2.reshape(-1,60); yf = yw2.ravel()\n    mask = np.ones(10*T,bool)\n    for s in range(10): mask[s*T:s*T+15]=False\n    A = np.column_stack([np.ones(mask.sum()), Xf[mask]]); b = yf[mask]\n    coef,_,_,_ = np.linalg.lstsq(A,b,rcond=None)\n    res = b-A@coef\n    s2 = (res**2).sum()/(len(b)-A.shape[1])\n    cov = s2*np.linalg.pinv(A.T@A)\n    se = np.sqrt(np.maximum(np.diag(cov)[1:],1e-12))\n    t = coef[1:]/se\n    for j in range(60):\n        rows.append((prod, producers[j], coef[1+j], t[j], (prod,producers[j]) in bad_pairs))\nres = pd.DataFrame(rows, columns=['product','producer','beta','t','bad'])\nres.to_pickle(f'sim_t_{SEED}.pkl')\ninn = res[~res.bad]; bd = res[res.bad]\nprint('TRUE-BAD t: min=%.1f med=%.1f max=%.1f'%(bd.t.min(), bd.t.median(), bd.t.max()))\nprint('INNOCENT t quantiles:', int(round(np.median(E[s])/LAM0)))\n        state = np.zeros(N, dtype=int)  # 0 normal else recovery day 1..14\n        for day in range(1, T+1):\n            lam_base = E[s, day-1]/N\n            mult = mult_stage[state]\n            lam = lam_base*mult\n            dem = rng.poisson(lam)\n            D_syn[prod][day-1, s] = dem.sum()\n            # serving\n            lots = lots_by_cell.get((prod, s, day), [])\n            avail = sum(q for _,q,_ in lots)\n            if avail > 0:\n                order = rng.permutation(N)\n                # lot unit boundaries\n                bounds = []\n                acc = 0\n                for pr,q,whd in lots:\n                    bounds.append((acc, acc+q, (prod,pr) in bad_pairs))\n                    acc += q\n                pos_u = 0\n                rejected = []\n                for ci in order:\n                    if dem[ci] == 0: continue\n                    take = min(dem[ci], avail - pos_u)\n                    if take <= 0: break\n                    a, b = pos_u, pos_u+take\n                    is_bad = any(lo < b and a < hi and bad for lo,hi,bad in bounds)\n                    if is_bad and rng.random() < PREJ:\n                        rejected.append(ci)\n                    pos_u = b\n                for ci in rejected:\n                    state[ci] = 1   # will become day1 (0.2) tomorrow via mult index\n            # advance recovery\n            adv = state > 0\n            state[adv] += 1\n            state[state > 14] = 0\n\n# pipeline on synthetic\nU = {p: np.zeros((10, T+1, 60)) for p in products}\nfor (day, st, prod), lots in consumed.items():\n    for pr,q,whd in lots:\n        U[prod][st_idx[st], day, pr_idx[pr]] += q\n\nys, Es, zs_all = {}, {}, []\nfor prod in products:\n    y,E = build_y_from(D_syn[prod])\n    ys[prod], Es[prod] = y,E\n    zs_all.append((y*np.sqrt(E)).ravel())\nzs_all = np.concatenate(zs_all)\nc = 1.4826*np.median(np.abs(zs_all-np.median(zs_all)))\nrho_n=rho_d=0.0\nfor prod in products:\n    z = np.clip(ys[prod]*np.sqrt(Es[prod])/c,-3,3)\n    rho_n += (z[:,1:]*z[:,:-1]).sum(); rho_d += (z[:,:-1]**2).sum()\nrho = rho_n/rho_d\nprint('syn c=',round(c,3),'rho=',round(rho,3))\n\nrows=[]\nfor prod in products:\n    y,E = ys[prod], Es[prod]\n    w = np.sqrt(E)/c\n    yw = y*w; yw2 = yw.copy(); yw2[:,1:] -= rho*yw[:,:-1]\n    X = np.zeros((10,T,60)); u = U[prod]\n    for s in range(10):\n        un = u[s,1:,:]/E[s][:,None]\n        for li,k in enumerate(kernel):\n            X[s,1+li:,:] += k*un[:-(1+li)]\n        X[s] *= w[s][:,None]\n    X2 = X.copy(); X2[:,1:,:] -= rho*X[:,:-1,:]\n    Xf = X2.reshape(-1,60); yf = yw2.ravel()\n    mask = np.ones(10*T,bool)\n    for s in range(10): mask[s*T:s*T+15]=False\n    A = np.column_stack([np.ones(mask.sum()), Xf[mask]]); b = yf[mask]\n    coef,_,_,_ = np.linalg.lstsq(A,b,rcond=None)\n    res = b-A@coef\n    s2 = {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-fable-5","id":"msg_01PXrVNVgyxMdfagEGCznrVw","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Final answer written to `/app/answer.json` — 107 confirmed producer–product pairs (1–6 per product, every product has at least one).\n\n## How I got there\n\n**1. Exact supply-chain reconstruction (fully validated).**\nI parsed all four document formats, then simulated the wholesaler as a per-product FIFO queue: producers append stock in their daily receipt sequence, stores are fulfilled in their daily sequence. This model reproduced **all 3,650** store receipt totals exactly. Pushing the deliveries into per-store FIFO inventories (sales happen before same-day delivery arrives) reproduced the sales log perfectly — sold quantities never exceeded reconstructed stock, and lost sales occurred only on exact stockouts. So for every (day, store, product) I know precisely which producers' units were sold. I also found that consumer rows in `sales_log.csv` are in serving order (cumulative sold units map rows to lots exactly).\n\n**2. Rejection detection.**\nRejections are only observable as demand dips with the 20/50/80 staged-recovery shape starting the day after a bad purchase (next-day alignment fit clearly better than same-day). The demand model needed care: a product-specific calendar (cross-store robust median), a slowly drifting store-level baseline (iterative dip-masked rolling median — without this, a negative control produced artifacts as strong as real signals), and AR(1) prewhitening. I then regressed the whitened demand deficit jointly on all 60 producers' kernel-convolved, FIFO-derived exposures, per product.\n\n**3. Calibration of \"definitely\".**\nTwo independent nulls set the false-positive ceiling: (a) the identical pipeline run on demand *excess* (which no mechanism can produce) gave max t = 3.23 across 1,800 pairs; (b) a full forward simulation — real reconstructed flows, synthetic consumers with the staged-recovery mechanics and the strong offenders implanted — showed innocent producers never exceed t ≈ 2.6 even through spillover (innocent lots selling slowly through someone else's dip). I used **t ≥ 3.5** as the confirmation cutoff; everything above it is real with ≲1 expected false pair overall, and exposure-aligned deficits can only be produced by actual consumer rejections.\n\nTwo honest caveats: per-event logical attribution is impossible in this data — every detected rejection day's sales straddle 2–10 producers' lots (zero single-producer event days exist), so \"confirmed\" here means statistically certain at the producer level, pooled over each producer's ~180 yearly batches. And truly weak offenders (rejection effects below the noise floor) would be undetectable; the simulation suggests anything with a meaningful rejection rate clears the cutoff."}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":792,"cache_read_input_tokens":186977,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":792,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":34,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"9777808e-4b15-489a-a904-14dbfb0b648f","uuid":"6752cca6-a4eb-467b-bdb8-e4eebbf14012","request_id":"req_011CbwQnwVKcZ7bRUUTKQih3"}
